509	\subsection{Inductive-step substrate: per-gap decomposition of
510	  \texorpdfstring{\(\text{pd}(M) = k+1\)}{pd(M) = k+1}}
511	\label{subsec:succ_pd_gap_sequence}
512	
513	The inductive step
514	\cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}
515	of the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula is gated, as of the current pinned
516	Mathlib snapshot, by exactly three absent substrate ingredients
517	(gaps (1)--(3)) plus a fourth that is now in hand
518	(gap (4), \cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one}). This subsection records the
519	decomposition that the proof block of
520	\cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd} folds into four bullets:
521	its purpose is to make the dependency graph and the per-gap effort
522	estimates explicit, so that the substrate gaps can be formalised
523	independently (and where the dependency graph permits, in parallel).
524	
525	\paragraph{Sequencing constraint.} The three open gaps are
526	\emph{not} mutually independent at the substrate level. Gap (3)
527	(snake-lemma carving of the minimal resolution by an
528	\(M\)-regular element \(x \in \mathfrak m\)) needs gap (1)
529	(minimal-resolution normalisation: every finite free resolution of
530	a finite module over a Noetherian local ring trims to a minimal one
531	of the same projective dimension) as input, because the snake-lemma
532	output is only known to be a \emph{minimal} resolution of \(M/xM\)
533	over \(R/(x)\) of the same length when the input resolution is itself
534	minimal. Gap (2) (Stacks 00MF, the ``what is exact'' criterion in
535	terms of depths of \(r\)-minors of the transition matrices) is
536	independent of gaps (1) and (3) at the substrate level: it is a
537	self-contained criterion on a complex of finite free modules and
538	feeds the closure assembly without going through gap (1) or gap (3).
539	
540	\paragraph{Dependency diagram.} Read top-to-bottom as ``provides
541	input to'':
542	\begin{verbatim}
543	                gap (1)
544	       minimal-resolution carving
545	   (Stacks lemma-add-trivial-complex)
546	                  |
547	                  v
548	                gap (3)
549	        snake lemma on minimal
550	        resolution under x in m
551	                  |
552	                  v
553	   +--------------+--------------+
554	   |   L1299 closure assembly    |   <--   gap (2)
555	   |   (succ-pd inductive step)  |        Stacks 00MF
556	   +-----------------------------+        "what is exact"
557	                  ^
558	                  |
559	                gap (4)
560	       depth drops by one (CLOSED;
561	       see lem:depth_drops_by_one)
562	\end{verbatim}
563	Gap (4) is already formalised
564	(\cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one}, the project's
565	\texttt{depth\_quotSMulTop\_succ\_eq\_depth\_of\_isSMulRegular})
566	and feeds the closure assembly directly. Gap (2) feeds the closure
567	assembly independently of the gap (1) \(\to\) gap (3) chain.
568	
569	\paragraph{Per-gap effort estimates.} The following table records,
570	for each gap, the estimated formalisation effort (lines of code in
571	the project's Lean source style), a coarse iter budget, the
572	Mathlib-at-\texttt{b80f227} status, and substrate dependencies on
573	other gaps:
574	\begin{center}
575	  \begin{tabular}{|c|l|c|c|c|c|}
576	    \hline
577	    Gap & Statement                              & LOC est.  & Iters
578	        & Mathlib status     & Depends on \\
579	    \hline
580	    (1) & Minimal-resolution carving             & 40--80    & 1--2
581	        & per-syzygy step CLOSED iter-199
582	                                                                & none \\
583	    (2) & Stacks 00MF (``what is exact'')        & 150--200  & 2--3
584	        & absent             & none \\
585	    (3) & Snake lemma on minimal resolution      & ---       & ---
586	        & \textbf{OBVIATED iter-200} & --- \\
587	    (4) & Depth drops by one                     & ---       & ---
588	        & \textbf{CLOSED}    & --- \\
589	    \hline
590	  \end{tabular}
591	\end{center}
592	The gap (1) estimate is anchored to Bruns--Herzog,
593	\emph{Cohen--Macaulay Rings}, Construction~1.5.18 together with the
594	minimal-resolution induction (\S1.5): the trimming algorithm is a
595	finite-rank linear-algebra computation followed by induction on the
596	length of the resolution. The gap (2) estimate reflects the chain
597	Bruns--Herzog Theorem~1.4.13 \(\to\) Eagon--Northcott
598	\(\to\) Buchsbaum--Eisenbud's exactness criterion (Stacks 00MF /
599	\texttt{proposition-what-exact}), which characterises exactness of a
600	complex of finite free modules in terms of depths of the ideals of
601	\(r\)-minors of the transition matrices. Gap (2) is the largest of
602	the three open substrates and is a natural candidate for an upstream
603	Mathlib contribution rather than a project-internal proof.
604	
605	\paragraph{Gap (3) OBVIATED iter-200.} The iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB
606	pivot (\ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}) replaces the literal
607	\(\texttt{ChainComplex}\,\mathbb N\) carrier with the abstract
608	\(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) SES-descent pattern; under this
609	pattern, minimality of the resolution is \emph{not} used as input
610	anywhere in the inductive step. Consequently the snake-lemma carving
611	of the minimal resolution (gap (3)) is no longer a substrate
612	requirement: only gap (2) (\(\texttt{pd}\,M > 0 \Rightarrow
613	\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)) remains the binding gap for
614	the closure of \ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}, and the
615	per-syzygy step of gap (1) is consumed only through the SES at
616	\(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) (no minimality input required).
617	See \ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot} for the SES-descent details.
618	
619	\paragraph{Gap (2) Stacks 00MF proof recipe.} The
620	Buchsbaum--Eisenbud exactness criterion (Stacks 00MF /
621	\texttt{proposition-what-exact}) reads: a complex
622	\(\dots \to R^{r_2} \xrightarrow{\varphi_2} R^{r_1}
623	 \xrightarrow{\varphi_1} R^{r_0}\) of finite free modules over a
624	Noetherian local ring \(R\) is exact at \(R^{r_i}\) (for \(i \geq 1\))
625	if and only if \(\texttt{rank}\,\varphi_{i+1} + \texttt{rank}\,\varphi_i
626	= r_i\) and \(\texttt{depth}\,I(\varphi_i) \geq i\), where
627	\(I(\varphi)\) denotes the ideal of \(r\)-minors of \(\varphi\) for
628	\(r = \texttt{rank}\,\varphi\). The recipe for an iter-201+ prover:
629	(a) define \(I(\varphi) \subseteq R\) as the ideal of all
630	\(\texttt{rank}\,\varphi\)-minors of \(\varphi\) (Mathlib supports
631	this via \(\texttt{Matrix.minors}\) and \(\texttt{Ideal.span}\) over the
632	finite index set of \(\binom{n}{r}\) tuples); (b) prove the depth
633	lower bound via induction on \(i\) using a Koszul-complex argument
634	on the regular sequence implicit in the exactness data; (c) derive
635	\(\texttt{pd}\,M > 0 \Rightarrow \texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)
636	as a corollary by taking the start \(\dots \to R^{r_1} \to R^{r_0} \to
637	M\) of a minimal resolution and applying the criterion at \(i = 1\).
638	Estimated \(\sim 150-200\) LOC project-side; Mathlib upstream PR
639	candidate.
640	
641	\paragraph{Closure assembly (iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot).} The
642	iter-200 substrate landing in \ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}
643	replaces the original (1)+(3)-dependent assembly with an SES-descent
644	recipe: given an \(M\) with \(\texttt{pd}_R(M) = k+1\), the
645	per-syzygy step of gap (1)
646	(\ref{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}) supplies a
647	surjection \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\); the SES
648	\(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) plus the iter-200 helpers
649	\(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ker\_of\_surjection}\) +
650	\(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_succ\_of\_hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ker}\)
651	give \(\texttt{pd}_R(\ker f) = k\) exactly; gap (2) (the
652	Buchsbaum--Eisenbud criterion at the start of a minimal resolution)
653	delivers \(\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\); the iter-200
654	helper
655	\(\texttt{depth\_ker\_ge\_min\_of\_surjection\_finite\_localRing}\)
656	gives \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f \geq \min(\texttt{depth}\,R,
657	\texttt{depth}\,M + 1) = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\); a complementary LES
658	upper bound (or the IH applied to \(\ker f\) under the strict
659	descent of \(\texttt{pd}\)) gives the matching upper bound. Combine
660	to get \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\), then apply
661	the IH (\(\texttt{pd}\,\ker f = k\)) for
662	\(k + \texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,R\), which rearranges
663	to \((k+1) + \texttt{depth}\,M = \texttt{depth}\,R\) as required.
664	Estimated closure-assembly cost (gaps in hand): \(\sim 50-80\) LOC
665	inductive code.
666	
667	\paragraph{Iter budget refinement.} Aggregating the per-gap
668	estimates with the closure assembly: gap (1) at 1--2 iters, gap (2)
669	at 2--3 iters, gap (3) at 1--2 iters, and the closure assembly
670	at 1 iter gives a total remaining iter budget of \textbf{5--8 iters}
671	for \cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}. The schedule may
672	parallelise across the gap (2) branch and the gap (1) \(\to\) gap (3)
673	chain --- so wall-clock could be as low as
674	\(\max(\text{gap (2)},\, \text{gap (1)} + \text{gap (3)}) +
675	\text{closure}\), i.e.\ \(\max(2\text{--}3,\, 2\text{--}4) + 1\),
676	yielding 3--5 iters in the parallel-best case. The substrate
677	sequencing constraint (gap (3) requires gap (1)) is hard; the gap
678	(2) branch is genuinely independent of that chain and could be
679	dispatched as a parallel prover lane the moment a free slot opens.
680	This estimate refines the earlier ``~6--12 iters'' coarse budget
681	recorded in \texttt{STRATEGY.md}: the refined window is somewhat
682	tighter on the upper end (5--8 vs.\ 6--12 sequential) and is
683	substantially tighter under parallel dispatch (3--5 parallel-best
684	vs.\ 6--12).

686	\subsection{Gap (1) first-step substrate (iter-199): per-syzygy
687	  minimal surjection from a finite free module}
688	\label{subsec:ab_gap1_first_step}
689	
690	\begin{lemma}
691	
692	\leanok
693	  [Per-syzygy minimal surjection from \(R^n\) for finite modules
694	    over a local ring]
695	  \label{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}
696	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}
697	  \uses{def:depth}
698	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LK
699	  % (lemma-add-trivial-complex; minimal-resolution carving substrate)
700	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L24590-L24623).
701	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
702	  % "Suppose $R$ is a ring. Let
703	  %     \ldots
704	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i + 1}}
705	  %     R^{n_i}
706	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_i}
707	  %     R^{n_{i-1}}
708	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i-1}}
709	  %     \ldots
710	  %  be a complex of finite free $R$-modules. Suppose that for some $i$
711	  %  some matrix coefficient of the map $\varphi_i$ is invertible.
712	  %  Then the displayed complex is isomorphic to the direct sum of a
713	  %  complex
714	  %     \ldots \to
715	  %     R^{n_{i + 2}} \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i + 2}}
716	  %     R^{n_{i + 1}} \to
717	  %     R^{n_i - 1} \to
718	  %     R^{n_{i - 1} - 1} \to
719	  %     R^{n_{i - 2}} \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i - 2}}
720	  %     R^{n_{i - 3}} \to
721	  %     \ldots
722	  %  and the complex $\ldots \to 0 \to R \to R \to 0 \to \ldots$ where
723	  %  the map $R \to R$ is the identity map."
724	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LK
725	    (\texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}).}
726	  Let \(R\) be a local ring (Noetherian-ness NOT required at this
727	  step), \(\mathfrak m \subseteq R\) the maximal ideal,
728	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\) the residue field, and \(M\) a finite
729	  \(R\)-module. Then there exist \(n \in \mathbb N\) and an
730	  \(R\)-linear surjection \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\) such that:
731	  \begin{enumerate}
732	    \item \(n = \dim_\kappa(\kappa \otimes_R M)\) (the minimal
733	      generator count of \(M\), via Nakayama);
734	    \item \(\ker f \subseteq \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\) (the
735	      ``minimality'' condition: every entry of every relation lives
736	      in the maximal ideal, so the chosen lift has no trivial
737	      summands).
738	  \end{enumerate}
739	\end{lemma}
740	\begin{proof}
741	  Pick a \(\kappa\)-basis
742	  \(b : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \kappa \otimes_R M\) via
743	  \texttt{Module.finBasis} with
744	  \(n = \dim_\kappa(\kappa \otimes_R M)\). Surjectivity of the
745	  canonical map \(M \twoheadrightarrow \kappa \otimes_R M\)
746	  (composition of \texttt{TensorProduct.mk\_surjective} and
747	  \texttt{Ideal.Quotient.mk\_surjective}) gives lifts
748	  \(m_i \in M\) with \(1 \otimes m_i = b_i\). Define
749	  \(f : R^n \to M\) by \(f x := \sum_i x_i \cdot m_i\) via
750	  \texttt{(Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m}.
751	
752	  Surjectivity: \(\mathrm{range}\, f = \mathrm{span}_R(m_i)\) by
753	  \texttt{Module.Basis.constr\_range}, and
754	  \(\mathrm{span}_R(m_i) = M\) by Nakayama via
755	  \texttt{IsLocalRing.span\_eq\_top\_of\_tmul\_eq\_basis}.
756	
757	  Kernel containment: for \(x \in \ker f\) the relation
758	  \(\sum_i x_i m_i = 0\) tensors to
759	  \(\sum_i (1 \otimes x_i \cdot m_i) = 0\); using
760	  \texttt{TensorProduct.tmul\_smul} and the linear independence of
761	  \(b\) (\texttt{Module.Basis.linearIndependent}) we obtain
762	  \(\mathrm{residue}_R(x_i) = 0\) for every \(i\), i.e.\
763	  \(x_i \in \mathfrak m\). Decomposing
764	  \(x = \sum_i x_i \cdot \mathrm{Pi.single}\,i\,1\) then yields
765	  \(x \in \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\) by
766	  \texttt{Submodule.smul\_mem\_smul} and
767	  \texttt{Submodule.sum\_mem}.
768	\end{proof}
769

771	minimal-resolution carving: given a finite \(R\)-module \(M\)
772	over a local ring \(R\), it produces a minimal surjection

797	/-! ### Helper iter-193 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth is preserved under R-linear equivalence
798	
799	For a commutative ring `R`, an ideal `I ⊆ R`, and two `R`-modules `M, M'` with
800	an `R`-linear equivalence `e : M ≃ₗ[R] M'`, we have `depth I M = depth I M'`.
801	
802	This is the standard "depth is an invariant of the isomorphism class" fact;
803	the proof has two steps: (1) the side-condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved
804	under linear equivalence, and (2) the regular-sequence supremum sets agree
805	via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Both halves are direct Mathlib calls; the
806	helper is axiom-clean.
807	
808	iter-193 Lane G structural advance: this helper is the substrate piece
809	needed to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)` for `M` finite free, which
810	in turn closes the `pd(M) = 0` base case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
811	formula modulo the `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` direct-sum step (a separate
812	substrate item; the file's residual sorries are narrowed to that one
813	named piece). -/
814	lemma depth_eq_of_linearEquiv {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
815	    {M M' : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
816	    (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M') :
817	    depth I M = depth I M' := by
818	  -- Step 1: `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved under the linear equivalence.
819	  have hcond : (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤) ↔ (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤) := by
820	    have e_top : Submodule.map (e : M →ₗ[R] M') (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
821	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e
822	    have e_symm_top :
823	        Submodule.map (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M) (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤ := by
824	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e.symm
825	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
826	    · intro h
827	      have hmap :=
828	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M) (e : M →ₗ[R] M')
829	      rw [h, e_top] at hmap
830	      exact hmap.symm
831	    · intro h
832	      have hmap :=
833	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M') (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M)
834	      rw [h, e_symm_top] at hmap
835	      exact hmap.symm
836	  -- Step 2: the `sSup` sets agree via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`.
837	  unfold depth
838	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤
839	  · simp [if_pos h, if_pos (hcond.mp h)]
840	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt hcond.mpr h)]
841	    congr 1
842	    ext n
843	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
844	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
845	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mp hreg⟩
846	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
847	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mpr hreg⟩

849	/-! ### Helper iter-194 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth of constant Pi equals depth of fiber.
850	
851	For a commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`,
852	`depth I (ι → M) = depth I M`. The proof goes through the regular-sequence
853	characterization: each `r`-action on `ι → M` is pointwise (so an `r ∈ R` is
854	regular on `ι → M` iff regular on `M`), and the quotient `(ι → M)/r·⊤`
855	identifies with `ι → M/r·⊤` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
856	`I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.
857	
858	This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
859	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (where `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` via a basis). -/
860	
861	/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `ι`, and module `M`,
862	the ideal-action `I • ⊤_{ι → M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I • ⊤_M`s. -/
863	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
864	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
865	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
866	    (I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))) =
867	      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set ι) (fun (_ : ι) => I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) := by
868	  apply le_antisymm
869	  · intro f hf i _
870	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
871	    · intro a hain x _
872	      change a • x i ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)
873	      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
874	    · intro x y hx hy
875	      change (x + y) i ∈ _
876	      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
877	  · intro f hf
878	    rw [show f = ∑ j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
879	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
880	    intro j _
881	    have hfj : f j ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
882	    have hmap :
883	        Pi.single j (f j) ∈
884	          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : ι) => M) j)
885	            (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
886	      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨f j, hfj, rfl⟩
887	    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
888	    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap
889	
890	/-- The side condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on `ι → M` and `M` for nonempty
891	finite `ι`: a free product of fibre `I•⊤_M`-witnesses combines to a
892	`I•⊤_{ι → M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
893	`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
894	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
895	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
896	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
897	    I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤ ↔ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
898	  constructor
899	  · intro h
900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by
905	      rw [h]; trivial
906	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
907	    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
908	    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
909	  · intro h
910	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
911	    intro f _ i _
912	    rw [h]
913	    trivial
914	
915	/-- `QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] ι → QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `ι`,
916	obtained by rewriting `r • ⊤ = Ideal.span {r} • ⊤` and using
917	`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
918	private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
919	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (r : R)
920	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
921	    QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] (ι → QuotSMulTop r M) := by
922	  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)

924	  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
925	  congr 1
926	  funext _
927	  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ⊤

930	is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to

936	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
937	    (rs : List R) :
938	    ∀ {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M],
939	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (ι → M) rs ↔
940	        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs := by
941	  induction rs with
942	  | nil =>
943	    intro M _ _
944	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
945	    · rintro ⟨_, hPi_top⟩

948	      intro habs

951	      intro f _
952	      funext i
953	      exact habs (f i) trivial
954	    · rintro ⟨_, hM_top⟩
955	      refine ⟨.nil R (ι → M), ?_⟩

957	      obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
958	      intro habs

961	      intro m _

968	    refine and_congr ?_ ?_
969	    · constructor
970	      · intro h
971	        obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›

973	      · intro h

989	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)

1000	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩

1002	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1003	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mpr hreg⟩

1005	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): depth drops by exactly one for a regular
1006	element (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).
1007	
1008	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
1009	`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:
1010	`depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`.
1011	
1012	This is one of the four substrate gaps for the Auslander–Buchsbaum n=k+1 step (the
1013	"depth-drops-by-one" piece). The proof packages the long-exact sequence of
1014	`Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`, using that
1015	`[x]` is zero on every `Ext^*(κ, M)` (since `x ∈ Ann κ`) to break the LES into short

1018	Routed through `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on both sides + the `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`

1020	lemma depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular
1021	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1022	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1023	    [Nontrivial M] {x : R} (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1024	    (hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x) :
1025	    depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 =
1026	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
1027	  haveI hMxM_nt : Nontrivial (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1028	    nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem

1125	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): regular-element existence from positive depth.
1126	
1127	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
1128	`1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ∈ 𝔪`. This
1129	is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
1130	(Nakayama rules out the `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
1131	reading off a length-1 witness.

1133	This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
1134	two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
1135	element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
1136	lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
1137	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1138	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1139	    [Nontrivial M]
1140	    (h : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
1141	    ∃ x : R, x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular M x := by
1142	  -- Unfold depth: `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
1143	  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
1144	    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
1145	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
1146	  have hdepth_unfold :
1147	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1148	        = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1149	          (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1150	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1151	    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
1152	  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
1153	  have hlt : (0 : ℕ∞) < sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1154	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1155	      ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1156	    calc (0 : ℕ∞) < 1 := by norm_num
1157	      _ ≤ _ := h
1158	  obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
1159	  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ℕ∞) < ↑rs.length).
1160	  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
1161	    by_contra h_neg
1162	    push Not at h_neg
1163	    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp h_neg
1164	    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
1165	    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
1166	    rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
1167	    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
1168	  rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
1169	  · simp at hrs_pos
1170	  · refine ⟨x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_⟩
1171	    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1

1173	/-! ### Helper iter-199 Lane AB-gap1 (axiom-clean): minimal surjection substrate.
1174	
1175	For a finite `R`-module `M` over a local ring `R`, there exists a surjective
1176	`R`-linear map `f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M` of the **minimal possible rank**
1177	`n = dim_κ (κ ⊗_R M)` (where `κ = R/𝔪` is the residue field) whose **kernel
1178	is contained in `𝔪 • ⊤`**. This is the first step of constructing a *minimal
1179	finite free resolution*: iterating the construction on the kernel (which is
1180	itself finitely generated when `R` is Noetherian) produces successive
1181	syzygies whose differential maps each have image in `𝔪` times their target.
1182	
1183	This is the first substrate piece of gap (1) (Stacks
1184	`lemma-add-trivial-complex`) in the Auslander–Buchsbaum closure chain
1185	(`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`). It packages the basic
1186	**Nakayama-lift** of a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M` to an `R`-spanning family in `M`
1187	and reads off the kernel-containment from linear independence of the basis
1188	combined with the `1 ⊗_R -` evaluation.
1189	
1190	Mathlib substrate used (all axiom-clean):
1191	* `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` — Nakayama lift of a κ-basis.
1192	* `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` — the `1 ⊗_R -` map is surjective for the
1193	  residue-field tensor.
1194	* `Module.Basis.constr_range` — range of the linear extension equals span of
1195	  the chosen image set.
1196	* `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` — independence of a κ-basis.
1197	* `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` — `r ∈ 𝔪 ↔ residue r = 0`. -/
1198	lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
1199	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1200	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
1201	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
1202	      Function.Surjective f ∧
1203	      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1204	        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ∧
1205	      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
1206	  set κ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hκ
1207	  set n := _root_.Module.finrank κ (TensorProduct R κ M) with hn
1208	  -- Pick a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M`.
1209	  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (TensorProduct R κ M) :=
1210	    _root_.Module.finBasis κ (TensorProduct R κ M)
1211	  -- The canonical map `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` is surjective.
1212	  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
1213	    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
1214	    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1215	  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
1216	  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
1217	  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
1218	  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
1219	  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n → R` to `m i`.
1220	  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
1221	  -- Evaluation: `f x = Σ x i • m i`.
1222	  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
1223	    intro x
1224	    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
1225	        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
1226	    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
1227	      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
1228	    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
1229	    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
1230	  -- Range = span of `m`.
1231	  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
1232	    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
1233	  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
1234	  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
1235	    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
1236	  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
1237	  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
1238	  · -- Kernel containment in `𝔪 • ⊤`.
1239	    intro x hx
1240	    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
1241	    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
1242	    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.
1243	    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R κ M) := by
1244	      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
1245	    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
1246	    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ⊗_R (x i • m i) = residue(x i) • b i`.
1247	    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
1248	        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
1249	      intro i
1250	      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1251	          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
1252	        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
1253	      rw [hm i]; rfl
1254	    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1255	        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
1256	      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
1257	    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
1258	    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
1259	    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
1260	      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
1261	        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
1262	      exact fun i => this i
1263	    -- Convert each component-in-𝔪 to `x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
1264	    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
1265	      intro i
1266	      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
1267	      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
1268	    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
1269	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
1270	    intro i _
1271	    have hsingle :
1272	        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
1273	          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
1274	      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
1275	    rw [hsingle]
1276	    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial

1354	For a surjection `f : R^n →ₗ M` from a finite free module of rank `n ≥ 1` over a
1355	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, with both `M` and `ker f` nontrivial, the depth

1358	  min(depth_𝔪 R, depth_𝔪 M + 1) ≤ depth_𝔪 (ker f).
1359	```
1360	This is Stacks 00LE (3) (`depth_of_short_exact` part 3) applied to the
1361	SES `0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0`, combined with `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`
1362	to identify `depth(R^n)` with `depth(R)`.
1363	
1364	This is the ≥ direction of the LES analysis on the SES; the matching ≤ direction
1365	(`depth(ker f) ≤ min(depth R, depth M + 1)`) is the residual gap needed for the
1366	inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula. It requires either the "what
1367	is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) or a refined LES argument when
1368	`depth M < depth R`; the current substrate provides only the easy direction. -/
1369	lemma depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing
1370	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1371	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
1372	    {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
1373	    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
1374	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
1375	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
1376	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
1377	  -- Set up instances on the auxiliary modules.
1378	  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ⟨⟨0, hn⟩⟩
1379	  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ⟨default⟩
1380	  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
1381	  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
1382	  -- The SES: 0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0.
1383	  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
1384	    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
1385	  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
1386	    Subtype.val_injective
1387	  -- Apply `depth_of_short_exact` part (3).
1388	  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
1389	  have h3 := htriple.2.2
1390	  -- Rewrite `depth (Fin n → R) = depth R`.
1391	  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n → R)
1392	      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
1393	    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
1394	  rw [heq] at h3
1395	  exact h3
1396	
1397	end Module

1399	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1400	
1401	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1402	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1403	```
1404	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1405	```
1406	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1407	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1408	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1409	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1410	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1411	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1412	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1413	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1414	
1415	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/

1435	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1436	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1437	
1438	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1439	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1440	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1441	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1442	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1443	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1444	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1445	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1446	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1447	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1448	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1449	
1450	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1451	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1452	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1453	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1454	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1455	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1456	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1457	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1458	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1459	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1460	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1461	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.

1660	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
1661	
1662	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
1663	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
1664	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
1665	
1666	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
1667	
1668	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
1669	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
1670	
1671	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
1672	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
1673	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
1674	this is the upstream gap-fill.
1675	
1676	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
1677	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
1678	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
1679	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
1680	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1681	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
1682	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
1683	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
1684	  depth_eq_krullDim :
1685	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
1686	
1687	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
1688	
1689	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
1690	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
1691	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
1692	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
1693	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
1694	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
1695	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
1696	
1697	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
1698	
1699	namespace CohenMacaulay
1700	
1701	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
1702	
1703	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
1704	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
1705	specialisation of the equality
1706	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
1707	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
1708	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
1709	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
1710	
1711	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
1712	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
1713	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1714	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
1715	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
1716	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
1717	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
1718	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
1719	    intro habs
1720	    apply h.top_ne_smul
1721	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
1722	    rw [habs]; simp
1723	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
1724	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
1725	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
1726	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
1727	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
1728	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq

1747	/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`
1748	
1749	**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
1750	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
1751	```
1752	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
1753	```
1754	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
1755	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
1756	
1757	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
1758	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
1759	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
1760	`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
1761	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
1762	
1763	**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
1764	1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
1765	   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
1766	   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
1767	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
1768	2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
1769	   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
1770	   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
1771	   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
1772	3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
1773	   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
1774	4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
1775	   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.

1777	   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
1778	   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by
1779	   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, which
1780	   reduces the κ-finrank to spanFinrank (a ring-theoretic invariant) and

1783	**iter-187 deliverable**: structural scaffold with the assembled body left
1784	as a single named typed sorry (the κ-subspace identification step). The
1785	preparatory lemma `toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq` lands axiom-clean.

1788	* `Ideal.mapCotangent`, `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq`,
1789	  `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)

1791	* `finrank_span_singleton` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)

2041	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
2042	
2043	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
2044	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
2045	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
2046	
2047	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
2048	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
2049	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
2050	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
2051	   `lemma-one-equation`).
2052	
2053	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
2054	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
2055	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
2056	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
2057	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
2058	
2059	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
2060	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
2061	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
2062	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
2063	
2064	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
2065	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
2066	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
2067	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
2068	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
2069	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
2070	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
2071	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
2072	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
2073	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
2074	helper closes. -/

2076	/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain
2077	
2078	The Auslander–Buchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay"
2079	ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
2080	is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose
2081	this implication (`lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is a domain"
2082	returns nothing relevant; `lean_loogle` for `IsRegularLocalRing _ → IsDomain _`
2083	returns empty).
2084	
2085	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
2086	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
2087	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
2088	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
2089	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
2090	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
2091	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
2092	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
2093	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
2094	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
2095	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
2096	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
2097	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
2098	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
2099	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
2100	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
2101	
2102	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
2103	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter
2104	or Mathlib upstream contribution. -/
2105	
2106	/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
2107	with `spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1`, there exists `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
2108	
2109	This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪²`
2110	then `𝔪 = 0` (so `spanFinrank 𝔪 = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
2111	private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
2112	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2113	    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
2114	    ∃ x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
2115	  -- By contradiction: assume 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪², then by Nakayama 𝔪 = 0, but spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1.
2116	  by_contra h_neg
2117	  push Not at h_neg
2118	  have h𝔪_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
2119	  -- 𝔪² = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
2120	  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
2121	      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
2122	    rw [sq, ← Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
2123	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
2124	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
2125	  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ⊥.
2126	  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ≤ (⊥ : Ideal R).jacobson :=
2127	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
2128	  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
2129	      ≤ ⊥ ⊔ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) •
2130	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
2131	    rw [bot_sup_eq, ← hsq]; exact h𝔪_le_sq
2132	  have h𝔪_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ≤ ⊥ :=
2133	    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot h𝔪_fg hjac h_le_smul
2134	  have h𝔪_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ⊥ := le_bot_iff.mp h𝔪_bot
2135	  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
2136	    rw [h𝔪_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
2137	  omega

2264	/-! ### Helper iter-191 Lane G (axiom-clean): zero-divisor witness from a minimal prime
2265	
2266	For a commutative ring `R` and a minimal prime `𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R`, every
2267	element of `𝔭` is a zero-divisor in `R`. Concretely: for any `x ∈ 𝔭`, there
2268	exists `y ∈ R, y ≠ 0` with `x * y = 0`.
2269	
2270	Proof: minimal primes are disjoint from non-zero-divisors via
2271	`Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes`, so `x ∈ 𝔭` ⟹
2272	`x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R` ⟹ `∃ y ≠ 0, x * y = 0`.
2273	
2274	This packages the Stacks-00NQ-relevant first step for the `x ∈ 𝔭` case of
2275	`isDomain_of_regularLocal`: when `(x)` is a minimal prime, `x` is a zero-divisor,
2276	so the obstruction reduces to derivation of a contradiction from the
2277	zero-divisor witness (using the regular-local structure of `R` and `R/(x)`). -/
2278	private lemma exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes
2279	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {𝔭 : Ideal R} (h𝔭 : 𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R)
2280	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ 𝔭) :
2281	    ∃ y : R, y ≠ 0 ∧ x * y = 0 := by
2282	  have hdisj : Disjoint (𝔭 : Set R) (nonZeroDivisors R : Set R) :=
2283	    Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes h𝔭
2284	  have hxNot : x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R := fun hxNZD =>
2285	    (Set.disjoint_left.mp hdisj) hx hxNZD
2286	  have hExistsZD : ¬ ∀ z, x * z = 0 → z = 0 := fun h => hxNot <| by
2287	    rw [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff]
2288	    refine ⟨h, fun z hz => h z (by rw [mul_comm]; exact hz)⟩
2289	  push Not at hExistsZD
2290	  obtain ⟨y, hxy, hy⟩ := hExistsZD
2291	  exact ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩

2542	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = n → IsDomain R by

2565	      (I := (⊥ : Ideal R)) (J := Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) bot_le

2684	    exists_isSMulRegular_notMemSq_of_regularLocal_succ (k := k) hdim
2685	  refine ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, ?_⟩
2686	  -- Step 2: assemble the structural instances on `R/(x)`.

2689	  have hspan_ne_top : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≠ ⊤ :=

2691	  haveI : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=

2701	    rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
2702	    exact hdim
2703	  have hR'_cot_eq :
2704	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
2705	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by

2748	/-- **Lane G inductive step (iter-185 structural scaffold).** Packages the
2749	inductive step of Stacks 00OD: given a regular local ring `R` of dimension
2750	`k + 1`, plus the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k` (universally
2751	quantified in the ring), produce a regular sequence of length `k + 1` in
2752	the maximal ideal of `R`.

2754	The body is **axiom-clean** modulo the typed-`sorry` substrate helper
2755	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`. Assembly path:
2756	
2757	1. Helper extracts `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular R x` AND `IsRegularLocalRing
2758	   (R⧸(x))` of `spanFinrank = k`.
2759	2. IH applied on `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` produces a regular sequence
2760	   `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` of length `k` in the maximal ideal there.
2761	3. Lift `rs'_q` to `rs : List R` via `Function.surjInv` of
2762	   `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the section property gives
2763	   `rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) = rs'_q`.
2764	4. Members of `rs` lie in `𝔪` because the maximal ideal of `R⧸(x)` is the
2765	   image of `𝔪` (`IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal` applied to the comap chain).
2766	5. Cons via `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'` to form the length-`(k+1)`
2767	   sequence `x :: rs`.

2769	This factoring isolates the Mathlib gap to one narrow typed `sorry` instead of
2770	spreading it across the lemma body; future iterations can attack Stacks 00NQ
2771	+ 00NU directly. -/
2772	private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2773	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2774	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2775	    (IH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
2776	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
2777	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k →
2778	            ∃ rs : List R', rs.length = k ∧
2779	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ∧
2780	              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
2781	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ∧
2782	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
2783	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
2784	  -- Step 1: extract `x ∈ 𝔪` regular on `R` with `R ⧸ (x)` regular local of
2785	  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
2786	  obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
2787	    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
2788	  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ⧸ (x)` — this gives a regular sequence of length
2789	  -- `k` on the quotient, valued in its maximal ideal.
2790	  obtain ⟨rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q⟩ := IH (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot
2791	  -- Step 3: lift `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` to `rs' : List R` via the right
2792	  -- inverse of the (surjective) quotient ring hom.
2793	  let mkq : R →+* R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
2794	  let g : R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2795	  have hg : ∀ y, mkq (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
2796	  let rs' : List R := rs'_q.map g
2797	  have hlen_rs' : rs'.length = k := by simp [rs', hlen_q]
2798	  have hmkmap : rs'.map mkq = rs'_q := by
2799	    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
2800	    rw [List.map_map]
2801	    conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id rs'_q]
2802	    exact List.map_congr_left fun y _ => hg y
2803	  -- Step 4: each element of `rs'` lies in `𝔪 R` via the comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))`.
2804	  -- The maximal ideal of `R ⧸ (x)` comaps back to `𝔪 R` (it's the *unique*
2805	  -- maximal ideal of `R` containing `Ideal.span {x} ⊆ 𝔪`).

2807	    intro r hr
2808	    simp only [rs', List.mem_map] at hr
2809	    obtain ⟨y, hy_mem, rfl⟩ := hr
2810	    -- The comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))` under the surjective `mkq` is a maximal ideal
2811	    -- of `R`; since `R` is local, it equals `𝔪 R`.  Hence `g y ∈ comap mkq 𝔪'`
2812	    -- iff `g y ∈ 𝔪 R`.
2813	    have hmax_comap : (Ideal.comap mkq
2814	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
2815	      Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2816	    have heq : Ideal.comap mkq
2817	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
2818	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
2819	      (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax_comap
2820	    rw [← heq, Ideal.mem_comap]
2821	    exact (hg y).symm ▸ hmem_q y hy_mem
2822	  -- Step 5: cons `x` onto `rs'` to form the length-`(k+1)` regular sequence.
2823	  refine ⟨x :: rs', ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
2824	  · simp [hlen_rs']
2825	  · intro r hr
2826	    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr_in
2827	    · exact hxMem
2828	    · exact hmem_rs' r hr_in
2829	  · -- `IsRegular R (x :: rs')` via `IsRegular.cons'`.
2830	    -- Need `IsSMulRegular R x` (have `hxReg`) AND

12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	
14	Given an abelian category `C`, a SES `0 → K → P → M → 0` where `P` is a
15	finite product of a generator-like object `R`, a "test object" `κ`, and a
16	derived-functor invariant `depth(X) := inf{i : Ext^i(κ, X) ≠ 0}`, recover an
17	exact equality `depth(K) = depth(M) + 1` from the LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)`. The
18	upper bound `depth(K) ≤ depth(M) + 1` is the residual difficulty (the lower
19	bound is the standard "snake-into-LES" assembly). The upper bound boils
20	down to: at the index `i = depth(M)`, the LES connecting map
21	`δ : Ext^i(κ, M) → Ext^{i+1}(κ, K)` must be injective, which is equivalent
22	to the map immediately preceding it, `Ext^i(κ, P) → Ext^i(κ, M)`, being
23	the zero map at the relevant index.
24	
25	## Failed approaches (from directive)
26	
27	- **Full minimal free resolution as a `ChainComplex ℕ`**: 3-4× over budget,
28	  blocked on a Mathlib-absent termination-of-syzygy-tower lemma.
29	- **Stacks 090V classical induction on depth(M)**: explicitly retired.
30	- **Direct AB formula proof in Mathlib's library**: Mathlib does not ship
31	  `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` axiom-clean.
32	
33	## Analogues found
34	
35	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` + `_₂` + `_₃` (LES of `Ext^*(X, -)`) — the project's own `depth_of_short_exact` already wires it (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:676–795`)
36	
37	- **Domain**: derived-functor LES in any abelian category; the project's
38	  consumer is `RingTheory.Module` over local Noetherian R.
39	- **Same structural problem there**: the LES injectivity argument the
40	  project uses for `depth_of_short_exact (3)` (the LOWER bound `min(depth N,
41	  depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'`) is *exactly* the same shape as the desired
42	  UPPER bound `depth(ker f) ≤ depth(M) + 1`. The difference is the index
43	  at which one inspects the LES.
44	- **Technique**: at index `i = depth(M)`, isolate the LES fragment
45	  `Ext^i(κ, P) →^{·g} Ext^i(κ, M) →^{δ} Ext^{i+1}(κ, K) →^{·f} Ext^{i+1}(κ, P)`.
46	  If `Ext^i(κ, P) → Ext^i(κ, M)` is the zero map, then `δ` is injective on
47	  the nonzero source `Ext^i(κ, M) ≠ 0`, forcing `Ext^{i+1}(κ, K) ≠ 0`, i.e.
48	  `depth(K) ≤ i + 1 = depth(M) + 1`.
49	  Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂` already
50	  exposes the existential `x_2.comp(g_*) = 0 ⇒ ∃ x_1, x_1.comp(f_*) = x_2`;
51	  this is the "injectivity of δ via vanishing of the predecessor" packaged
52	  as the project's `depth_of_short_exact` helpers use it.
53	- **Mapping to project**: instantiate the LES at `P = R^n`, `K = ker f`,
54	  the SES from `exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing`, the test object
55	  `κ = R/𝔪`. The `Ext^i(κ, R^n) → Ext^i(κ, M)` map collapses *because of
56	  minimality* — see the next analogue.
57	- **Porting cost**: low (already wired). The same `covariant_sequence_exact₂`
58	  call pattern used in `depth_of_short_exact` part (1) gives the LES
59	  injectivity. Cost is in the "the predecessor map is zero" lemma.
60	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
61	
62	### Analogue: `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (project-side, Stacks 00LP, `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:229–254`)
63	
64	- **Domain**: Ext/derived-functor scalar action — the κ-vector-space
65	  structure on `Ext^*(κ, -)`.
66	- **Same structural problem there**: "for any `x ∈ Ann(N)` and any
67	  `e ∈ Ext^i(N, M)`, `x • e = 0`". This is the abstract Stacks 00LP fact
68	  *the project already proved axiom-clean*. It powers the iter-198 closure of
69	  `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`.
70	- **Technique**: scalar `x ∈ R` acts on `Ext^i(κ, M)` via precomposition
71	  by `mk₀(x · 𝟙_κ)`. For `x ∈ 𝔪 = Ann(κ)`, `x · 𝟙_κ = 0` in
72	  `ModuleCat`, so `mk₀ … = mk₀ 0 = 0` and `0.comp e = 0`. The same trick
73	  applies to **postcomposition**: by R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` (i.e.
74	  `Ext.bilinearCompOfLinear` in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.Linear`),
75	  `e.comp (mk₀(x · 𝟙_M))` = `x • (e.comp (mk₀ 𝟙_M))` = `x • e`, and the
76	  identical annihilator argument yields zero.
77	- **Mapping to project**: this is the *engine that makes the "predecessor
78	  map is zero" lemma true*. The "predecessor map" `Ext^i(κ, R^n) → Ext^i(κ, M)`
79	  is induced by `f : R^n → M`. By minimality (the existing
80	  `exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing` lemma), the matrix `A` of
81	  the kernel inclusion `K ≅ R^m ↪ R^n` has entries in `𝔪`. The
82	  postcomposition by `A` (i.e. `Ext^i(κ, A) : Ext^i(κ, R^m) → Ext^i(κ, R^n)`)
83	  is then the zero map, by the matrix-decomposition route:
84	  `A = ∑_{i,j} A_{ij} • E_{ij}` (sum over basis matrices, scalars in 𝔪);
85	  the Ext bilinearity (`Ext.add_comp` + `Ext.smul_comp`) distributes;
86	  each summand has a factor `A_{ij} ∈ Ann(κ)` acting on
87	  `Ext^i(κ, R^n)`, which by `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` is zero.
88	  Plug this "predecessor map is zero" into the LES injectivity argument
89	  above to close the base case `pd M = 1 ⇒ depth M < depth R`.
90	- **Porting cost**: medium. The matrix-decomposition helper is ~30-60 LOC
91	  (basis-matrix decomposition of an R-linear map, finite-sum distributivity
92	  of `Ext.comp`, invoking the existing `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`).
93	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
94	
95	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.bilinearComp` / `bilinearCompOfLinear` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.{Basic,Linear}`)
96	
97	- **Domain**: bilinearity of derived-functor composition.
98	- **Same structural problem there**: `Ext.comp` is `R`-bilinear, with the
99	  named lemmas `Ext.add_comp`, `Ext.comp_add`, `Ext.smul_comp`,
100	  `Ext.comp_smul`, and the bundled forms
101	  `Ext.bilinearComp` (AddCommGroup-bilinear) and
102	  `Ext.bilinearCompOfLinear` (`R`-bilinear).
103	- **Technique**: rewrite `Ext.comp` as a bilinear pairing
104	  `Ext X Y a →ₗ[R] Ext Y Z b →ₗ[R] Ext X Z (a+b)` and apply the standard
105	  "sum and scalar-multiply commute with the pairing" lemmas. This is
106	  exactly the machinery needed to push the matrix decomposition through
107	  `Ext^i(κ, A)`.
108	- **Mapping to project**: the `Ext.add_comp` + `Ext.smul_comp` lemmas are
109	  the building blocks of the "predecessor map is zero" lemma above. No
110	  additional Mathlib infrastructure is needed beyond what is already
111	  imported transitively.
112	- **Porting cost**: low (Mathlib provides the lemmas; the use site is the
113	  ~30-60 LOC matrix-collapse helper described above).
114	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
115	
116	### Analogue: `Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.Module`)
117	
118	- **Domain**: structure of projectives over local rings.
119	- **Same structural problem there**: a finite projective module over a
120	  local ring is free (since projective ⟹ flat, and flat + finite over
121	  local ⟹ free).
122	- **Technique**: standard structure result; no novel technique to port.
123	- **Mapping to project**: in the base case `pd M = 1`, the kernel `K` of
124	  the minimal surjection has `pd K = 0`, hence projective, hence (being
125	  finite over a local ring) free. This is what makes `K ≅ R^m` available
126	  in the base case, which is required for the matrix-decomposition step.
127	  Direct Mathlib import — no project-side build needed.
128	- **Porting cost**: low (just invoke `Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing`
129	  via `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
130	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
131	
132	### Discarded analogues
133	
134	- **`Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Regular` (depth-via-dim machinery)**:
135	  the project-side `Module.depth` is built on `Ext`-indices, not on
136	  `ringKrullDim` arithmetic. The KrullDim-side lemmas do not interface
137	  with the project's `depth` predicate without an `IsCohenMacaulay`-style
138	  bridge that the project explicitly is *building*, not consuming. Dropped.
139	- **Sheaf-cohomology LES injectivity (`Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.SheafCondition.LocalPredicate`)**:
140	  structurally identical (`δ` injective when preceding map is zero) but
141	  the technique boils down to the same `covariant_sequence_exact` shape
142	  already in hand. No additional value beyond the project's existing
143	  Ext-LES wiring.
144	- **Group-cohomology LES (`Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.GroupCohomology`)**:
145	  same structural shape, same technique, no portable surplus.
146	- **Cohen-Macaulay depth-comparison short-cut**: the project's
147	  `CohenMacaulay` predicate (defined this file, §6) is itself downstream
148	  of the AB formula being closed; using `[CohenMacaulay R]` to side-step
149	  the closure would be circular. Dropped.
150	
151	## Top suggestion
152	
153	**Adopt Path B (LES-injectivity route) rather than Path A (standalone
154	Stacks 00MF substrate).** The closure splits into:
155	
156	1. **Inductive step** `pd M = k + 1`, `k ≥ 1` (~50-80 LOC): apply
157	   `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection` + the descent-non-LT
158	   contrapositive (via `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`)
159	   to get `pd K = k` *exactly*. IH gives `k + depth K = depth R`, hence
160	   `depth K < depth R` (subtraction in ℕ∞ is "honest" because `k ≥ 1` and
161	   `depth R ≥ k`). Lower-bound `depth M ≥ depth K - 1` from
162	   `depth_of_short_exact` part (2). Upper-bound `depth M ≤ depth K - 1` by
163	   contradiction: assume `depth M ≥ depth K`, inspect the LES at
164	   `i = depth K`, conclude `Ext^{depth K}(κ, R^n)` injects from
165	   `Ext^{depth K}(κ, K) ≠ 0`, so `depth R ≤ depth K`, contradicting IH.
166	
167	2. **Base case** `pd M = 1` (~80-120 LOC): `K = ker f` is finite
168	   projective over local Noetherian R, hence free
169	   (`Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing` via
170	   `Module.Flat.of_projective`). Pick a basis to get `K ≅ R^m` (m ≥ 1
171	   since M is not projective). The inclusion `K ↪ R^n` corresponds to an
172	   `(n × m)`-matrix `A` whose entries lie in `𝔪` (from minimality —
173	   `ker f ≤ 𝔪 • ⊤`). Build the helper:
174	
175	   > **Lemma** (matrix-collapse): For `A : R^m → R^n` with all entries
176	   > in `𝔪 = Ann(κ)`, the postcomposition map
177	   > `(- ∘ mk₀ A) : Ext^i(κ, R^m) → Ext^i(κ, R^n)` is zero.
178	
179	   Proof: `A = ∑_{i,j} A_{ij} • E_{ij}` (basis-matrix decomposition);
180	   by `Ext.add_comp` + `Ext.smul_comp` (Mathlib's bilinear-comp infra),
181	   `Ext^i(κ, A) = ∑_{i,j} A_{ij} • Ext^i(κ, E_{ij})`. Each summand has a
182	   scalar factor `A_{ij} ∈ Ann(κ)` acting on `Ext^i(κ, R^n)`, which by
183	   the *existing* helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` is zero.
184	   Hence the whole sum is zero. ~50-80 LOC.
185	
186	   With matrix-collapse in hand, the LES at `i = depth R - 1` gives
187	   `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, M) → Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) → Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^n)`
188	   with the second map equal to `Ext(κ, A) = 0`. If `depth M ≥ depth R`
189	   the source vanishes, so `Ext^{depth R}(κ, K)` would have to be zero —
190	   but `K ≅ R^m`, `m ≥ 1`, so `depth K = depth R` and
191	   `Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) ≠ 0`. Contradiction. Hence `depth M < depth R`.
192	
193	**Total Path B LOC estimate: ~130-200 LOC**, comparable to Path A but
194	without committing to a generic Buchsbaum-Eisenbud / Stacks 00MF
195	predicate that has no other downstream consumer in the project. The
196	binding new helper is the matrix-collapse lemma, which is purely a
197	linear-algebraic decomposition + the existing `ext_smul_…annihilator`
198	trick.
199	
200	First Mathlib file to read for matrix-decomposition idioms:
201	`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.ToLin` (matrix ↔ linear map between
202	finite-free modules) and
203	`Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.Linear` (R-linearity of
204	`Ext.comp`). First project file to touch: extend
205	`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` around line 1290
206	(the `RingTheory.Module` namespace with the existing `HasProjectiveDimensionLT`
207	helpers) with the matrix-collapse lemma, then expand
208	`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` (L1517) to take the inductive +
209	base-case branches and close the `sorry` at L1574.

/-- Convenience shortcut for `ModuleCat.hom_bijective.injective`. -/
lemma hom_injective {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} :
    Function.Injective (Hom.hom : (M ⟶ N) → (M →ₗ[R] N)) :=
  hom_bijective.injective

/-- Convenience shortcut for `ModuleCat.hom_bijective.surjective`. -/
lemma hom_surjective {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} :
    Function.Surjective (Hom.hom : (M ⟶ N) → (M →ₗ[R] N)) :=
  hom_bijective.surjective

@[simp]
lemma hom_ofHom {X Y : Type v} [AddCommGroup X] [Module R X] [AddCommGroup Y]
    [Module R Y] (f : X →ₗ[R] Y) : (ofHom f).hom = f := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ofHom_hom {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) :
    ofHom (Hom.hom f) = f := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ofHom_id {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] : ofHom LinearMap.id = 𝟙 (of R M) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ofHom_comp {M N O : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup O] [Module R M]
    [Module R N] [Module R O] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] O) :
    ofHom (g.comp f) = ofHom f ≫ ofHom g :=
  rfl

/- Doesn't need to be `@[simp]` since `simp only` can solve this. -/
lemma ofHom_apply {M N : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R M] [Module R N]
    (f : M →ₗ[R] N) (x : M) : ofHom f x = f x := rfl

lemma inv_hom_apply {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} (e : M ≅ N) (x : M) : e.inv (e.hom x) = x := by
  simp

lemma hom_inv_apply {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} (e : M ≅ N) (x : N) : e.hom (e.inv x) = x := by
  simp

/-- `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` bundled as an `Equiv`. -/
def homEquiv {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} : (M ⟶ N) ≃ (M →ₗ[R] N) where
  toFun := Hom.hom
  invFun := ofHom

/-- For a linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` with image in `I • ⊤`, every entry
`A (Pi.single j 1) i` is in I. -/
lemma matrix_entry_mem_of_image_in_smul_top
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ} (I : Ideal R)
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R))
    (hA_im : LinearMap.range A ≤ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (Fin n → R)))
    (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ I := by
  have h : A (Pi.single j 1) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (Fin n → R)) :=
    hA_im (LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _)
  sorry

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) :
    I • (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (I : Submodule R R) := by
  simp

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

/-- For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n`, write A as a sum of "elementary"
maps `h_ij` weighted by matrix entries. -/
lemma linearMap_finFunR_sum_decomp
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj ij.2) := by
  ext x i
  -- Goal: A x i = (∑ ij, ...) x i
  -- RHS unfolds to: ∑ ij, ((A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • h_ij) x i
  --              = ∑ ij, A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • (Pi.single ij.1 (x ij.2)) i
  --              = ∑ ij, A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • (if i = ij.1 then x ij.2 else 0)
  --              = ∑_j, A (Pi.single j 1) i • x j
  -- We want: A x i = (A x) i = ∑_j x j • A (Pi.single j 1) i
  -- (since A x = ∑_j x j • A(Pi.single j 1) by linearity)
  simp only [LinearMap.sum_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp,
    LinearMap.coe_single, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
    smul_eq_mul]
  -- Want: A x i = ∑ ij, A(Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 * Pi.single ij.1 (x ij.2) i
  -- Simplify the Pi.single: Pi.single ij.1 (x ij.2) i = if i = ij.1 then x ij.2 else 0
  -- After collapsing this, sum reduces to ∑_j A(Pi.single j 1) i * x j
  sorry

input.code:
import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

/-- For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n`, write A as a sum of "elementary"
maps `h_ij` weighted by matrix entries. -/
lemma linearMap_finFunR_sum_decomp
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj ij.2) := by
  classical
  ext x i
  -- LHS: A x i
  -- RHS: sum over ij of A_ij * (Pi.single ij.1 (x ij.2)) i
  have hRHS :
      (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
        (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
          (LinearMap.single R (fun _ => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj ij.2)) x i =
      ∑ j : Fin m, A (Pi.single j 1) i * x j := by
    simp only [LinearMap.sum_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp,
      LinearMap.coe_single, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.smul_apply]
    -- Need to fold the sum over Fin n × Fin m to a sum over Fin m by using
    -- that Pi.single ij.1 (x ij.2) i is nonzero only when ij.1 = i.
    rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single i]
    · rw [Pi.single_eq_same]; ring
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb]
      simp
    · intro h
      exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ i) h
  rw [hRHS]
  -- LHS: A x i, where A x = ∑_j (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1)
  have hAx : A x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • A (Pi.single j 1) := by
    conv_lhs => rw [show x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • Pi.single j (1 : R) from ?_]
    · rw [map_sum]
      refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
      rw [map_smul]
    · ext j
      simp [Pi.single_eq_same]
      rw [Finset.sum_eq_single j]
      · simp
      · intro b _ hb
        simp [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm]
      · intro h
        exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ j) h
  rw [hAx]
  simp only [Finset.sum_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
  ring

universe u

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ} :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ⟨0, sorry⟩ ∘ₗ
    @LinearMap.proj R (Fin m) _ (fun _ => R) _ _ ⟨0, sorry⟩

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) (j : Fin m) (i : Fin n) :
    (A (Pi.single j 1) i) •
      ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
        (@LinearMap.proj R (Fin m) _ (fun _ => R) _ _ j)) :
        (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) =
      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) •
      (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
        (LinearMap.proj j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) := by
  rfl

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

/-- The matrix decomposition: for an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n`,
A can be written as a sum over (i,j) of A_ij • h_ij where h_ij are
"elementary" maps. -/
lemma linearMap_finFunR_sum_decomp
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) := by
  classical
  ext x i
  -- Step 1: compute the RHS at (x, i).
  have hRHS :
      (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
        (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
          (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
            (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R))) x i =
      ∑ j : Fin m, A (Pi.single j 1) i * x j := by
    simp only [LinearMap.sum_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp,
      LinearMap.coe_single, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.smul_apply]
    rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    -- Goal: ∑ b : Fin n, A_{b,j} • Pi.single b (x j) i = A_{i,j} * x j
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single i]
    · rw [Pi.single_eq_same]; rw [smul_eq_mul]
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb]
      simp
    · intro h
      exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ i) h
  rw [hRHS]
  -- Step 2: compute A x i = ∑_j (x j) * (A (Pi.single j 1)) i.
  have hsplit : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • Pi.single j (1 : R) := by
    ext k
    simp only [Finset.sum_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
    · simp
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm]; ring
    · intro h
      exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
  conv_lhs => rw [hsplit, map_sum]
  simp only [Finset.sum_apply, map_smul, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
  ring

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

lemma linearMap_finFunR_sum_decomp
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) := by
  classical
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro x
  apply funext
  intro i
  have hRHS :
      (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
        (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
          (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ

  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]

  have : ∀ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      ((A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R))) (Pi.single j 1) =

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

lemma linearMap_finFunR_sum_decomp
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) := by
  classical
  apply LinearMap.pi_ext'
  intro j
  apply LinearMap.ext_ring
  -- Goal: (A ∘ₗ Single j) 1 = (RHS ∘ₗ Single j) 1
  -- Both sides are vectors in Fin n → R. Show they're equal by funext.
  funext i
  -- Goal: (A ∘ₗ Single j) 1 i = (RHS ∘ₗ Single j) 1 i
  -- Push the ∘ₗ inside
  show A (Pi.single j 1) i = (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R))) (Pi.single j 1) i
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  -- Goal: A (Pi.single j 1) i = (∑ ij, (A_{ij}) • h_{ij} (Pi.single j 1)) i
  rw [Finset.sum_apply]
  -- Goal: A (Pi.single j 1) i = ∑ ij, ((A_{ij}) • h_{ij} (Pi.single j 1)) i
  rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product]
  -- Goal: A (Pi.single j 1) i = ∑ i', ∑ j', (...) i
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single i]
  · -- inner: ∑ j' : Fin m, ...
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single j]
    · -- (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • h_{ij} (Pi.single j 1) i with i' = i, j' = j
      show A (Pi.single j 1) i = ((A (Pi.single j 1) i) •
        ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j 1) : Fin n → R)) i
      -- (proj j) (Pi.single j 1) = 1
      -- single i 1 = Pi.single i 1
      -- (A_{ij}) • Pi.single i 1
      -- at i: A_{ij}
      show A (Pi.single j 1) i = ((A (Pi.single j 1) i) •
        (Pi.single i ((Pi.single j (1 : R)) j) : Fin n → R)) i
      rw [Pi.single_eq_same]
      show A (Pi.single j 1) i =
        (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) i
      rw [Pi.single_eq_same]
      ring
    · intro b _ hb
      show ((A (Pi.single b 1) i) •
        ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj b : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j 1) : Fin n → R)) i = 0
      show ((A (Pi.single b 1) i) •
        (Pi.single i ((Pi.single j (1 : R)) b) : Fin n → R)) i = 0
      rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm]
      simp
    · intro h
      exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ j) h
  · intro i' _ hi'
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero]
    intro j' _
    show ((A (Pi.single j' 1) i') •
      ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i' ∘ₗ
        (LinearMap.proj j' : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j 1) : Fin n → R)) i = 0
    show ((A (Pi.single j' 1) i') •
      (Pi.single i' ((Pi.single j (1 : R)) j') : Fin n → R)) i = 0
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hi']
    simp
  · intro h
    exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ i) h

lemma linearMap_finFunR_sum_decomp
