        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) := by
  classical
  apply LinearMap.pi_ext'
  intro j
  apply LinearMap.ext_ring
  funext i
  show A (Pi.single j (1 : R)) i = (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 (1 : R)) ij.1) •
        (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) ij.1 ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj ij.2 : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R))) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) i
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
  rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product]
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single i]
  · -- inner sum over j' : Fin m
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single j]
    · show A (Pi.single j (1 : R)) i =
        ((A (Pi.single j (1 : R)) i) •
          ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
            (LinearMap.proj j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) : Fin n → R)) i
      have h1 : (LinearMap.proj j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) = 1 := by
        change (Pi.single j (1 : R)) j = 1
        rw [Pi.single_eq_same]
      rw [show ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
            (LinearMap.proj j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) : Fin n → R) =
          LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i 1 from by rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]; congr 1; exact h1]
      rw [show LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i (1 : R) = Pi.single i (1 : R) from rfl]
      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
    · intro b _ hb
      show ((A (Pi.single b (1 : R)) i) •
        ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i ∘ₗ
          (LinearMap.proj b : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) : Fin n → R)) i = 0
      have h1 : (LinearMap.proj b : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) = 0 := by
        change (Pi.single j (1 : R)) b = 0
        rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm]
      rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, h1, map_zero, smul_zero, Pi.zero_apply]
    · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ j) h
  · intro i' _ hi'
    apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
    intro j' _
    show ((A (Pi.single j' (1 : R)) i') •
      ((LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i' ∘ₗ
        (LinearMap.proj j' : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R)) (Pi.single j (1 : R)) : Fin n → R)) i = 0
    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]
    rw [show (LinearMap.single R (fun _ : Fin n => R) i'
        ((LinearMap.proj j' : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R) (Pi.single j (1 : R))) : Fin n → R) =
        Pi.single i' ((LinearMap.proj j' : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] R) (Pi.single j (1 : R))) from rfl]
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hi', smul_zero]
  · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ i) h

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

-- Try a simpler decomposition: A = ∑_j (A_j) where A_j x = (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1)
-- This gives a sum over Fin m, with each summand a column.

private def colMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (A (Pi.single j 1))) ∘ₗ
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R) :
    colMap A j x = (x j) • A (Pi.single j 1) := by
  simp [colMap, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ j : Fin m, colMap A j := by
  classical
  apply LinearMap.pi_ext'
  intro k
  apply LinearMap.ext_ring
  -- Goal: (A ∘ₗ Single k) 1 = (∑ colMap A j ∘ₗ Single k) 1
  -- LHS = A (Pi.single k 1)
  -- RHS = ∑ j, colMap A j (Pi.single k 1) = ∑ j, (Pi.single k 1 j) • A (Pi.single j 1)
  --     = (Pi.single k 1 k) • A (Pi.single k 1) (others = 0)
  --     = A (Pi.single k 1)
  show A (Pi.single k (1 : R)) = ∑ j : Fin m, colMap A j (Pi.single k (1 : R))
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
  · simp [colMap, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply, Pi.single_eq_same]
  · intro b _ hb
    show colMap A b (Pi.single k (1 : R)) = 0
    simp [colMap, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm]
  · intro h
    exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

-- Element-by-element matrix decomposition.
private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
  classical
  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m → R) => ?_
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  -- Decompose A x = ∑_j (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1) (column decomposition)
  have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
    ext k
    rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
    · simp
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
    · intro h
      exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
  conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
  -- LHS = ∑ j, A ((x j) • Pi.single j 1) = ∑ j, (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1)
  -- RHS = ∑ (i,j), A_{i,j} • elemMap n m i j x = ∑ (i,j), A_{i,j} • Pi.single i (x j)
  -- (since elemMap n m i j x = ?)
  -- Let's compute elemMap n m i j x.
  have helem : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R),
      elemMap n m i j x = (Pi.single i ((x j) : R) : Fin n → R) := by
    intro i j x
    simp [elemMap, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
    -- (x j) • Pi.single i 1 = Pi.single i (x j)
    ext k
    rw [Pi.smul_apply]
    by_cases hk : k = i
    · subst hk
      rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
    · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hk), Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hk), smul_zero]
  -- Substitute helem in the RHS
  simp_rw [Pi.smul_apply, helem]
  -- Switch the order of sum: ∑ (i,j) = ∑ i (∑ j)
  rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
  -- Goal LHS: ∑ j, (x j) • A (Pi.single j 1)
  -- Goal RHS: ∑ j, ∑ i, (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • Pi.single i (x j)
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
  -- Want: (x j) • A (Pi.single j 1) = ∑ i, (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • Pi.single i (x j)
  -- Show by ext: at index k
  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
  -- LHS at k: (x j) • (A (Pi.single j 1) k) = (x j) * A_{k,j}
  -- RHS at k: ∑ i, ((A (Pi.single j 1) i) • Pi.single i (x j)) k = ∑ i, A_{i,j} • (Pi.single i (x j)) k
  --   = A_{k,j} • (x j)  (only i = k contributes)
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
  · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
  · intro b _ hb
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
  · intro h
    exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h

private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

-- Helper: r • Pi.single i 1 = Pi.single i r in Fin n → R.
lemma smul_pi_single_one {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (r : R) :
    r • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) = Pi.single i r := by
  classical
  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
  by_cases hk : k = i
  · subst hk
    rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R) :
    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
  simp [elemMap, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
  exact smul_pi_single_one _ _ _

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by

  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
    ext k

    · intro h

  have helem : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m),
      (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
    intro i j
    simp [elemMap, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
    exact smul_pi_single_one _ _ _
  simp_rw [Pi.smul_apply, helem]
  rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]

  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
  · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same]

  · intro b _ hb
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
  · intro h

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m)
    (x : Fin m → R) :
    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
  classical
  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R)
    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
  by_cases hk : k = i
  · subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]

example {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
  classical
  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m → R) => ?_
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  -- Show LHS and RHS both equal "∑ j, ∑ i, A_{i,j} • Pi.single i (x j)"
  rw [show A x = ∑ j : Fin m, ∑ i : Fin n,
      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • (Pi.single i (x j) : Fin n → R) from ?_]
  · -- RHS: ∑ ij, A_{ij.1, ij.2} • (elemMap ... ij.1 ij.2 x)
    --    = ∑ ij, A_{ij.1, ij.2} • Pi.single ij.1 (x ij.2)
    rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
  · -- Show A x = ∑ j, ∑ i, A_{ij} • Pi.single i (x j)
    -- LHS: A x. We have A x = ∑_j (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1) (by column decomposition of x).
    -- Then A(Pi.single j 1) = ∑_i (A(Pi.single j 1) i) • Pi.single i 1.
    -- So (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1) = ∑_i (A(Pi.single j 1) i) • (x j) • Pi.single i 1
    --                             = ∑_i (A(Pi.single j 1) i) • Pi.single i (x j).
    have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
      ext k
      rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
      · simp
      · intro b _ hb
        rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
      · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
    conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    rw [map_smul]
    -- (x j) • A(Pi.single j 1) = ∑ i, A(Pi.single j 1) i • Pi.single i (x j)
    ext k
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
    · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
    · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h

-- Test ofHom_smul

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

universe u

private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m)
    (x : Fin m → R) :
    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
  classical
  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R)
    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
  by_cases hk : k = i
  · subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]

lemma linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
  classical
  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m → R) => ?_
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  rw [show A x = ∑ j : Fin m, ∑ i : Fin n,
      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • (Pi.single i (x j) : Fin n → R) from ?_]
  · rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
    rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
  · have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
      ext k
      rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
      · simp
      · intro b _ hb
        rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
      · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
    conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    rw [map_smul]
    ext k
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
    · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
    · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h

-- Now the matrix-collapse Ext-level lemma.
lemma ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {N : ModuleCat.{u} R}
    {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R))
    (hA : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m), A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u))
    {p : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) p) :
    e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
              (ModuleCat.ofHom A : ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)))
          (add_zero p) = 0 := by
  -- key helper for the inner annihilator step
  have hAnnih : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (e' : Abelian.Ext.{u} N
        (ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)) p),
      A (Pi.single j 1) i • e' = 0 := by
    intro i j e'
    -- ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator applies to any Ext from N
    have : A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u) := hA i j
    have hkill : (A (Pi.single j 1) i • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
      apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
      apply LinearMap.ext
      intro n
      change A (Pi.single j 1) i • n = 0
      exact _root_.Module.mem_annihilator.mp this n
    have hreflect :
        (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (A (Pi.single j 1) i • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e'
          (zero_add p) = A (Pi.single j 1) i • e' := by
      have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (A (Pi.single j 1) i • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
                  : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
          = A (Pi.single j 1) i • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) (A (Pi.single j 1) i) (𝟙 N)
      rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
    rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
  -- Now decompose A as a sum
  rw [linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp A]
  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
      = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, ModuleCat.ofHom
          (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from ?_]
  · -- mk₀_sum
    rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_sum]
    -- comp_sum
    rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_sum]
    apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
    intro ij _
    -- ofHom_smul
    rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
        ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
        = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from ?_]
    · -- mk₀_smul
      rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R)]
      -- comp_smul
      rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_smul]
      exact hAnnih ij.1 ij.2 _
    · refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
      rfl
  · refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]
    rfl

    rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
  · have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by

    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :

    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]

          (ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) from
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) _ _]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_smul]
  exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _ (hA ij.1 ij.2)

lemma depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
    {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
  -- Set up instances on the auxiliary modules.
  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ⟨⟨0, hn⟩⟩
  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ⟨default⟩
  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
  -- The SES: 0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0.
  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
    Subtype.val_injective
  -- Apply `depth_of_short_exact` part (3).
  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
  have h3 := htriple.2.2
  -- Rewrite `depth (Fin n → R) = depth R`.
  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n → R)
      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
  rw [heq] at h3
  exact h3

end Module

lemma depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
    {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
  -- Set up instances on the auxiliary modules.
  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ⟨⟨0, hn⟩⟩
  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ⟨default⟩
  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
  -- The SES: 0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0.
  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
    Subtype.val_injective
  -- Apply `depth_of_short_exact` part (3).
  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
  have h3 := htriple.2.2
  -- Rewrite `depth (Fin n → R) = depth R`.
  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n → R)
      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
  rw [heq] at h3
  exact h3

/-! ### Helper iter-201 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): matrix decomposition and
matrix-collapse on `Ext`.

For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` between standard free modules over a
commutative ring R, A decomposes as `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` where
`A_{i,j} = (A (Pi.single j 1)) i` is the matrix entry and `E_{i,j}` is the
"elementary" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1`. From this
decomposition combined with R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` and the axiom-clean
helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`, we obtain the matrix-collapse
result: if every entry of A lies in `Ann_R N`, then the induced postcomposition
`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` via `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.

This is the key new substrate for closing the **Path B base case `pd M = 1`**
of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula: given a minimal surjection `f : R^n ↠ M`
with `ker f` free of positive rank, the inclusion `ker f ≅ R^k ↪ R^n` is an
R-linear map with entries in 𝔪 (by minimality `ker f ≤ 𝔪 • ⊤`); the
matrix-collapse then forces the LES injectivity needed to conclude
`depth M < depth R`. -/

/-- The "elementary matrix" linear map `E_{i,j} : R^m →ₗ R^n` sending
`Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1` and all other standard basis vectors to 0. -/
private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

/-- The elementary map `E_{i,j}` evaluated at `x : R^m` gives `Pi.single i (x j)`. -/
private lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ)
    (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R) :
    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
  classical
  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R)
    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
  by_cases hk : k = i
  · subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]

/-- Matrix decomposition: every R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` can be written
as a sum `∑_{(i,j)} A (Pi.single j 1) i • elemMap n m i j` of elementary maps
weighted by matrix entries. -/
private lemma linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
  classical
  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m → R) => ?_
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  rw [show A x = ∑ j : Fin m, ∑ i : Fin n,
      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • (Pi.single i (x j) : Fin n → R) from ?_]
  · rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
    rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
  · have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
      ext k
      rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
      · simp
      · intro b _ hb
        rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
      · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
    conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    rw [map_smul]
    ext k
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
    · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
    · intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
    · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h

/-- **Matrix-collapse on Ext.** For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` whose every
matrix entry `A (Pi.single j 1) i` lies in `Ann_R N`, the postcomposition map
`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` induced by `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.

Proof: write `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` via `linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp`.
Push through `ofHom`, `mk₀`, and `Ext.comp` using `ofHom_sum / mk₀_sum / comp_sum`
plus `ofHom_smul / mk₀_smul / comp_smul`. Each summand becomes
`A_{i,j} • (e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom (elemMap _ _ i j))))`, where the scalar `A_{i,j}`
lies in `Ann_R N`. The existing `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (Stacks
00LP fragment) makes each such scalar action zero. Hence the total sum is zero. -/
private lemma ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {N : ModuleCat.{u} R}
    {n m : ℕ}
    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R))
    (hA : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m),
        A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u))
    {p : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) p) :
    e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
              (ModuleCat.ofHom A :
                ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)))
          (add_zero p) = 0 := by
  classical
  rw [linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp A]
  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
      A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
      = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, ModuleCat.ofHom
          (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]
    rfl]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_sum]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_sum]
  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
  intro ij _
  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_; rfl]
  rw [show (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) :
        Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) (ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)) 0)
      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
          (ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) from
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) _ _]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_smul]
  exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _ (hA ij.1 ij.2)

end Module

  - `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul`, `Ext.comp_smul`
- **Verification:** `#print axioms` shows only the kernel triple
  `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd (line 1517, body NOT CLOSED)
- **Approach attempted (Path B from analogist):** With the matrix-collapse
  helper now in hand, the closure plan is:

  **Base case `k = 0` (pd M = 1)** — ~50-80 LOC residual:
  1. From `_hpd : projectiveDimension R M = 1`, derive
     `HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ker f) 1` for a minimal surjection
     `f : R^n →ₗ M` (using iter-200 helpers
     `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq` +
     `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection`).
  2. `K := ker f` is finite projective over local Noetherian R, hence free:
     `Module.Flat.of_projective` + `Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing`.
     Pick a basis to get an iso `φ : (Fin k → R) ≃ₗ[R] K`.
  3. Compose `A := K.subtype ∘ₗ φ.toLinearMap : (Fin k → R) →ₗ R^n`.
     Its image equals `K = LinearMap.ker f ⊆ 𝔪 • ⊤` (from minimality
     clause `_hf_min` of `exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing`).
  4. Show every entry `A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ 𝔪 = Ann_R κ`:
     - Use `ideal_smul_top_pi_const` (line 863) to identify
       `𝔪 • ⊤_{Fin n → R}` with the pointwise condition `∀ i, x i ∈ 𝔪`.
     - Apply at the column `A (Pi.single j 1) ∈ K ⊆ 𝔪 • ⊤`.
     - `Ideal.annihilator_quotient` then identifies `𝔪 = Ann_R κ`.
  5. Apply `ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator` (NEW)
     with `A` to get `e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom A)) = 0` for all
     `e : Ext^p(κ, R^k)`.
  6. Transport to the subtype: `subtype = A ∘ₗ φ.symm.toLinearMap`, so via
     `ofHom_comp` and `Ext.comp` associativity, the postcomposition map
     `Ext^p(κ, K) → Ext^p(κ, R^n)` (via the SES first morphism) is also zero.
  7. LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0`:
     `covariant_sequence_exact₂` at `i = depth R`: the map
     `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, M) →^δ Ext^{depth R}(κ, K)` has image equal to
     `ker(Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) → Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^n)) = Ext^{depth R}(κ, K)`
     (since the next map is zero). Together with `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, R^n) = 0`
     (depth `R^n = depth R > depth R - 1`), we get `δ` is an iso. Since
     `Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) = Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^k) ≠ 0` (K is free of rank k ≥ 1,
     depth K = depth R, so this Ext index doesn't vanish), we conclude
     `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, M) ≠ 0`, i.e., `depth M ≤ depth R - 1`,
     i.e., `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R`.
  8. The matching `depth M + 1 ≥ depth R` from `depth_of_short_exact`
     part (2): `min(depth R^n, depth K - 1) ≤ depth M`, i.e.,
     `min(depth R, depth R - 1) = depth R - 1 ≤ depth M` (when depth R ≥ 1).
  9. Combine for `depth M + 1 = depth R`. Edge case `depth R = 0`:
     the LES at `i = 0` gives `Ext^0(κ, K) = 0` (LHS is 0, RHS is 0 by matrix-collapse),
     but `Ext^0(κ, K) ≠ 0` since `depth K = 0`. Contradiction shows we cannot
     have `pd M = 1` and `depth R = 0` simultaneously.
  10. Also need to rule out `k = 0` (i.e., K = 0): if K = 0, then `f` is iso, so
      M is projective, so `pd M = 0`, contradicting `pd M = 1`.

  **Inductive step `k + 1, k ≥ 1`** — ~30-50 LOC residual:
  1. Same setup: minimal surjection f, get `HasProjectiveDimensionLT K (k+1)`.
  2. From `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`
     (contrapositive): `pd K = k` exactly.
  3. Apply main theorem `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` at `n = k` on K (NOT
     via `_succ_pd` k itself, but via the main theorem dispatching to
     `_succ_pd (k-1)` when k ≥ 1). [Needs `_succ_pd` to be structured as
     induction on k, generalizing M, for the IH to be available.]
  4. IH gives `k + depth K = depth R`, hence `depth K = depth R - k`.
  5. From step 4 + LES at `i = depth K` (using matrix-collapse on
     A : R^? → R^n where R^? is the iso of K = free of rank `R^?_rank`...
     wait — in this case K has pd k ≥ 1, K is NOT free. So matrix-collapse
     doesn't apply directly).
  6. **The inductive-step problem:** for k ≥ 1, K = ker f has pd k ≥ 1, so K
     is NOT projective. Matrix-collapse needs K to be free. So the inductive
     step needs a different argument.
  
  **Inductive step alternative (LES + IH only):**
  - The SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0` plus IH `k + depth K = depth R`.
  - `depth_of_short_exact (2)`: `min(depth R^n, depth K - 1) ≤ depth M`,
    i.e., `min(depth R, depth R - k - 1) = depth R - k - 1 ≤ depth M`
    (when `depth R ≥ k + 1`).
    So `depth M ≥ depth R - k - 1`, i.e., `depth M + k + 1 ≥ depth R`.
  - For the matching ≤: use `depth_of_short_exact (3)`:
    `min(depth R^n, depth M + 1) ≤ depth K = depth R - k`. So either
    `depth R ≤ depth R - k` (impossible for k ≥ 1) or
    `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R - k`, i.e., `depth M + k + 1 ≤ depth R`.
  - Combined: `depth M + k + 1 = depth R`, i.e., `(k+1) + depth M = depth R`.

  **So the inductive step k ≥ 1 closes via IH + `depth_of_short_exact`
  parts (2) and (3) alone — NO matrix-collapse needed.** The matrix-collapse
  is only used for the base case k = 0.

- **Result:** NOT ADDED (residual `sorry` at L1574 unchanged).
- **Why not landed this iter:** The base case requires ~50-80 LOC of LES
  bookkeeping + linear-equivalence transport on top of the matrix-collapse
  substrate. The inductive step requires restructuring `_succ_pd` to do
  Nat-induction on `k` (generalizing M) so the IH can be invoked at smaller k.
  Both pieces are doable in a follow-on iter with the matrix-collapse
  substrate in hand.
- **Next step:**
  - **iter-202 dispatch:** Close `_succ_pd` body. Restructure as
    `induction k generalizing M`; base case uses matrix-collapse + LES;
    inductive step uses IH + `depth_of_short_exact` parts (2) & (3).
  - Remove `private` per the iter-201 plan agent's option (1) commitment
    (blueprint NOTE resolution).

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

10	/-!
11	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
14	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
15	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
16	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
17	
18	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
19	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
20	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
21	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
22	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
23	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
25	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
26	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
27	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
28	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
31	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
32	  outside this iter's scope.
33	
34	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
35	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
36	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
37	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
38	
39	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
40	
41	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
42	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
43	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
44	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
45	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
46	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
47	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
48	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
49	
50	The 6 pinned declarations are:
51	
52	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
53	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
54	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
55	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
56	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
57	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
58	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
59	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
60	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
61	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
62	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
63	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
64	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
65	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
66	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
67	   header).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
107	
108	## References
109	
110	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` (~750 LOC,
111	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
112	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
113	-/

127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194	
195	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
196	
197	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
198	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
199	
200	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
201	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
202	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
203	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
204	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
205	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
206	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
207	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
208	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
209	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
210	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
211	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
212	
213	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
214	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
215	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
216	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
217	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
218	
219	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
220	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
221	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
222	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
223	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
224	
225	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
226	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
227	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
228	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
229	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
230	    [Smooth X.hom]
231	    (z : X.left) :
232	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
233	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
234	
235	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
236	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
237	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
238	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
239	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
240	
241	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
242	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
243	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
244	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
245	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
246	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
247	    [Smooth X.hom]
248	    (z : X.left) :
249	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
250	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
253	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z
254	
255	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
256	presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
257	existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
258	bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
259	localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
260	package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
261	`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
262	`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
263	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
264	localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.
265	
266	Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
267	`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.
268	
269	This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
270	"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
271	content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
272	Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
273	private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
274	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
275	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
276	    [Smooth X.hom]
277	    (z : X.left) :
278	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
279	      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
280	      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
281	      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
282	      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
283	      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
284	      IsLocalization.AtPrime
285	        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
286	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
287	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
288	  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
289	    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
290	  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
291	  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
292	  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
293	
294	/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
295	axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
296	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
297	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
298	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
299	basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
300	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.
301	
302	Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
303	+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
304	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
305	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
306	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
307	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
308	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
309	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
310	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
311	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
312	
313	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
314	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
315	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
316	`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
317	submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.
318	
319	Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
320	with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
321	step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
322	here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
323	chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
324	maneuver. -/
325	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
326	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
327	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
328	    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
329	    (M : Submonoid S)
330	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
331	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
332	    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
333	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
334	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
335	  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
336	    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
337	    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
338	
339	/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
340	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
341	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
342	submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
343	upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.
344	
345	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
346	(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
347	(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
348	provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
349	ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
350	Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
351	private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
352	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
353	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
354	    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
355	    (M : Submonoid S)
356	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
357	    [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
358	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
359	    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
360	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
361	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
362	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
363	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
364	  have hrank :
365	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
366	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
367	    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M
368	      (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
369	  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
370	        (S := S) n] at hrank
371	  simpa using hrank

373	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, RHS of the conormal iso).** Iter-198
374	Lane COE axiom-clean substrate helper: under the Stage 5a + 5b hypotheses
375	(`Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` is `Sₘ`-free with `Sₘ`-rank `n`), the residue-field base-change
376	`κ(mₘ) ⊗_{Sₘ} Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` has `κ(mₘ)`-dimension `n`. This is the *right-hand
377	side* of the Stacks-02JK cotangent iso
378	`κ(mₘ) ⊗_{Sₘ} Ω[Sₘ⁄R] ≃ mₘ/mₘ²` (the iso itself is the missing Mathlib
379	bridge for sub-lemma 6.B); combined with the bridge it gives
380	`Module.finrank κ (CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n`, the cotangent input of the
381	regularity criterion `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
382	
383	Axiom-clean: composes `Module.finrank_baseChange` (Mathlib re-export,
384	`LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Constructions.lean`) with the cardinal-to-natural
385	cast `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq`. Consumes no Mathlib gap.
386	
387	The hypothesis pattern (free + rank = n) is exactly the conclusion of
388	Stages 5a + 5b applied to the stalk of a smooth scheme; the helper is
389	phrased independently of `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` because the
390	stalk-side localisation is not preserved by that class (Mathlib commit
391	`b80f227` only provides `localization_away` which collapses to rel-dim 0).
392	-/
393	private theorem finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq
394	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
395	    {Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
396	    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])] (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
397	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
398	      (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
399	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange]
400	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
401	
402	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, RHS of the conormal iso) —
403	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`-tied form.** Iter-198 Lane COE
404	axiom-clean substrate helper, parallel to
405	`finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq` above but
406	with the rank hypothesis discharged directly from the
407	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R Sₘ` instance via Mathlib's

409	
410	This packaging is useful in the *closed-point* case where the
411	standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart `Spec S ⊆ X` is itself a
412	local ring (e.g. via Mathlib's `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`

414	stalk-as-localisation case, use the hypothesis-form helper above with
