417	discharging the `Free` + `rank = n` hypotheses respectively.
418	
419	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak
420	rank_kaehlerDifferential` (Mathlib),
421	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential` (Mathlib), and
422	`Module.finrank_baseChange` (Mathlib). Consumes no Mathlib gap. -/
423	private theorem
424	    finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
425	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
426	    {Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
427	    (n : ℕ) [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R Sₘ] :
428	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
429	      (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
430	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R Sₘ :=
431	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
432	  haveI : Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
433	  exact finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq n
434	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
435	      (R := R) (S := Sₘ) n)
436	
437	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, LHS-RHS bridge): formally-smooth residue
438	gives the cotangent iso.** Iter-199 Lane COE axiom-clean closed-point-style
439	helper: assuming the source ring `Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, its residue
440	field `κ = ResidueField Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, and `Ω[κ⁄R]` is
441	subsingleton (the three hypotheses combine to model the closed-point case of
442	Stacks 02JK over an algebraically closed base, where `κ = R` makes both FS
443	and `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` automatic), the canonical Mathlib map
444	`KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ`
445	is an `Sₘ`-linear equivalence between
446	`(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (the `Sₘ`-side packaging of `m/m²`)
447	and `κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` (the residue-field base-change of the Kähler
448	differentials).
449	
450	This is the closed-point case of the Stacks-02JK cotangent ↔ Kähler iso
451	(sub-gap (ii.A) of the Stage 6 chain in `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`).
452	The proof is the 3-step recipe from `analogies/coe-stacks02jk.md`:
453	
454	* **Step 1** — retraction → injection via
455	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`: under
456	  `FormallySmooth R κ` and surjectivity of `Sₘ → κ`, the conormal map
457	  `kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ` admits a left-inverse `l`, hence is injective.
458	* **Step 2** — `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection: combining
459	  `KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` with
460	  `Subsingleton (Ω[κ⁄R])` gives that every element of the tensor space lies
461	  in the kernel of `mapBaseChange`, hence in the range of `kerCotangentToTensor`.
462	* **Step 3** — bijective → iso: package as `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`.
463	
464	The `kerCotangentToTensor` map's domain is
465	`(RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent`, which equals
466	`(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent = IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ` since
467	`κ = Sₘ / maximalIdeal Sₘ`; the equivalence is therefore the
468	Stacks 02JK closed-point cotangent iso
469	`m/m² ≃ₗ[Sₘ] κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`.
470	
471	Axiom-clean: composes
472	`Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` (Mathlib;
473	`Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/Basic.lean`),
474	`KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` (Mathlib;
475	`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean`), and `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`. -/
476	private noncomputable def
477	    cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
478	    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
479	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
480	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
481	    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
482	    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
483	      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
484	  -- Surjectivity of `algebraMap Sₘ κ` is the residue surjection.
485	  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
486	      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
487	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
488	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
489	  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via `iff_split_injection`.
490	  have hInj : Function.Injective
491	      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
492	    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
493	      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
494	        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
495	    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
496	    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
497	    simpa using this
498	  -- Step 2: `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection.
499	  have hExact :=
500	    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
501	      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
502	  have hSurjMap :
503	      Function.Surjective
504	        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ
505	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
506	    intro x
507	    have h0 :
508	        KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ
509	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
510	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
511	    exact (hExact x).mp h0
512	  -- Step 3: bijective → linear equivalence.
513	  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

519	`Sₘ/m = κ`-module side of `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ`) as the domain

594	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), bundled bijective-residue-map form.**
595	Closed-point-friendly bundled variant of
596	`finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue` that replaces the three
597	typeclass hypotheses `Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)` and

605	
606	* `RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective` + `RingHom.formallySmooth_algebraMap`

615	private theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue
616	    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]

618	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
619	    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
620	    (hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)))

624	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) :=

629	  exact finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue n hrank

631	/-! ### Stage 6 sub-gap (ii.B) Stacks-00OE substrate (iter-200)
632	
633	The residual Mathlib gap for Stage 6 is the smooth-algebra Krull-dim formula:
634	for a standard-smooth `k`-algebra `S` of relative dim `n` and a maximal ideal
635	`m ⊂ S` corresponding to a closed point over an algebraically closed base,
636	`ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = n`.
637	
638	The iter-200 `mathlib-analogist coe-stacks00oe` recipe ranks this as a 3-step
639	chain composing existing Mathlib pieces:
640	
641	* **Step 1** (`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`, trivial):
642	  rewrite `ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = m.height`.
643	* **Step 2** (polynomial-ring height): for the standard-smooth presentation
644	  `MvPolynomial ι k ↠ S` with kernel a regular sequence, the contracted
645	  maximal ideal `m' ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k` has height `#ι`. This is built
646	  axiom-clean below via induction on `Fin n` along Mathlib's
647	  `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + `MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv`
648	  + `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing`.
649	* **Step 3** (regular-sequence drop, Stacks 00SW / 00OW): the relations of the
650	  standard-smooth presentation form a regular sequence in the localization,
651	  consumed via `ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`. The
652	  Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness is the substantive residual
653	  obligation; landing it requires `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.jacobian_isUnit`
654	  + `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`
655	  scaffolding which is iter-201+ Lane COE substrate.
656	
657	Iter-200 lands Step 2 fully axiom-clean (`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card`
658	+ general-finite-ι transport `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`),
659	the Step 1 named bridge (`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`), and
660	the Step 3 quotient-form re-export
661	(`ringKrullDim_quotient_isRegular_eq_sub_length`) so the iter-201+ closure
662	collapses to building the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness alone. -/
663	
664	/-- **Step 1 bridge (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** Ergonomic re-export of
665	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
666	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
667	translator inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` between
668	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 CoheightBridge bridge,
669	phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the Mathlib polynomial-ring height arithmetic
670	(phrased in `Ideal.height`).

673	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
674	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
675	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
676	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
677	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A
678	
679	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), `Fin n` form.** For a
680	field `k` and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`, the height of
681	`m` equals `n`.
682	
683	This is the substantive Step 2 ingredient: combined with the standard-smooth
684	presentation surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k ↠ S`, it computes the
685	height of any contracted maximal ideal `m' = preimage(m) ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k`
686	explicitly. The closed-point case is the substantive one — every maximal ideal
687	of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` has height exactly `n` (independent of whether `k`
688	is algebraically closed; the Jacobson property suffices).
689	
690	Axiom-clean proof: induction on `n`. The base case `n = 0` is trivial (no
691	maximal-ideal content beyond `bot_le`). The inductive step transports `m` along
692	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
693	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
694	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
695	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`
696	followed by the inductive hypothesis on `p`. The upper bound follows from
697	`Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top` + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`. -/
698	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
699	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
700	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
701	  induction n with
702	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
703	  | succ n ih =>
704	    intro m _
705	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
706	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
707	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
708	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'
709	    haveI : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing m'
710	    haveI : m'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
711	    have ih' := ih p
712	    have hh : m'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p m'
713	    have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e.toRingEquiv m).symm
714	    rw [hm_eq, hh, show ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; ring]
715	    gcongr

719	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) (hm : m ≠ ⊤) :
720	    m.height ≤ Nat.card ιx := by
721	  have h1 : (m.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial ιx k) :=
722	    Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hm
723	  rw [MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing, ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field,
724	    zero_add] at h1
725	  exact_mod_cast h1

727	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card
728	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ)
729	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [hmax : m.IsMaximal] :
730	    m.height = n := by
731	  apply le_antisymm
732	  · have := MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field k (ιx := Fin n) m hmax.ne_top
733	    simpa using this
734	  · exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field k n m

736	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), general `[Finite ι]` form.**
737	For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι`, and a maximal ideal
738	`m ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k`, the height of `m` equals `Nat.card ι`.
739	
740	Axiom-clean: transports the `Fin n` form
741	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card` along the canonical
742	`MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` induced by `(Finite.equivFin ι)`. -/
743	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard.{v}
744	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (ιx : Type v) [Finite ιx]
745	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal] :
746	    m.height = Nat.card ιx := by
747	  classical
748	  haveI : Fintype ιx := Fintype.ofFinite ιx
749	  let σ : ιx ≃ Fin (Fintype.card ιx) := Fintype.equivFin ιx
750	  let e : MvPolynomial ιx k ≃+* MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k :=
751	    (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k σ).toRingEquiv
752	  let m' : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k) := Ideal.map e m
753	  haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e)
754	  have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e m).symm
755	  have hcard : Nat.card ιx = Fintype.card ιx := by simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
756	  rw [hm_eq, hcard]
757	  exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card k (Fintype.card ιx) m'

773	`Sₘ = A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs`. -/
774	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial.{v}
775	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
776	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal]
777	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ιx k) A]
778	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
779	    ringKrullDim A = (Nat.card ιx : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
780	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A,

781	    MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard k ιx m]
782	  rfl

784	/-- **Step 3 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), parameterised additive form.**
785	For a Noetherian local ring `R'` with `ringKrullDim R' = a` (a natural number) and
786	a regular sequence `rs : List R'` of length `b`, the Krull dimension of the
787	quotient `R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus `b` equals `a`.
788

808	    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']
809	    (rs : List R') (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs)
810	    {a b : ℕ} (hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ℕ∞))

812	    ringKrullDim (R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) = (a : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
813	  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg
814	  rw [hDim, hLen] at h

1082	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
1083	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1084	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
1085	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1086	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1087	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1088	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1089	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
1090	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
1091	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
1092	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1093	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
1094	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
1095	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1096	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1097	  -- Regularity half: extracted to the named helper above
1098	  -- (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`), which captures the Stacks-00TT
1099	  -- Jacobian-criterion gap as a narrow named typed sorry. No further
1100	  -- inline `sorry` is required at this step.
1101	  have hreg : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
1102	    isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth X z
1103	  -- Krull-dim half: closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean CoheightBridge
1104	  -- equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
1105	  have hdim : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
1106	    rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
1107	    exact_mod_cast _hz
1108	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
1109	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
1110	  have hfin :
1111	      Module.finrank
1112	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
1113	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
1114	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
1115	    rw [hdim] at h
1116	    exact_mod_cast h
1117	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
1118	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
1119	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
1120	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
1121	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
1122	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
1123	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
1124	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.

1136	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
1137	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
1138	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
1139	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
1140	
1141	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
1142	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
1143	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
1144	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
1145	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
1146	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
1147	
1148	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
1149	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
1150	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
1151	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1152	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1153	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1154	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1155	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1156	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1157	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1158	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
1159	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1160	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1161	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1162	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1163	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
1164	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1165	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1166	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
1167	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
1168	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
1169	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
1170	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
1171	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
1172	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
1173	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
1174	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1175	    intro hF
1176	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
1177	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
1178	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
1179	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

1183	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1184	
1185	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1186	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1187	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1188	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1189	
1190	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1191	
1192	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1193	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1194	
1195	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1196	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1197	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1198	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1199	
1200	The proof combines two steps:
1201	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1202	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1203	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1204	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1205	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1206	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1207	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1208	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1209	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1210	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1211	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1212	
1213	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1214	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1215	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1216	
1217	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1218	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1219	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1220	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1221	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1222	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1223	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1224	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1225	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1226	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1227	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1228	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1229	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1230	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1231	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1232	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1233	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1234	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1235	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1236	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1237	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1238	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1239	  --
1240	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1241	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1242	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1243	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1244	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1245	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1246	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1247	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1248	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1249	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1250	  --
1251	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1252	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1253	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1254	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1255	  --
1256	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1257	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.

1260	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1261	
1262	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1263	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1264	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1265	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1266	
1267	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1268	
1269	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1270	codim 1.**
1271	
1272	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1273	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1274	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1275	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1276	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1277	
1278	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1279	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1280	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1281	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1282	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1283	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1284	
1285	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1286	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1287	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1288	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1289	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1290	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1291	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1292	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1293	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1294	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1295	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1296	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1297	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1298	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1299	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1300	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1301	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1302	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1303	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1304	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1305	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1306	  --
1307	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1308	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1309	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1310	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1311	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1312	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1313	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1314	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1315	  --   the domain of definition.
1316	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1317	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1318	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1319	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1320	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1321	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1322	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1323	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1324	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1325	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1326	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1327	  --   not packaged.
1328	  --
1329	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1330	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1331	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1332	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).

1335	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1336	
1337	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1338	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1339	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1340	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1341	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1342	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1343	witnesses are constructed in this project).

1347	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1348	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1349	
1350	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1351	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1352	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1353	
1354	which requires the pullback machinery
1355	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1356	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1357	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the

1361	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1362	
1363	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1364	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1365	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1366	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1367	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1368	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1369	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1370	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1371	the pullback machinery lands.
1372	
1373	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
1374	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/

1376	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
1377	
1378	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
1379	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
1380	are equivalent:
1381	
1382	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
1383	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
1384	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
1385	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.

1	# Analogy: Stacks 00SW — relations of a submersive presentation form a regular sequence at a closed-point localisation
2	
3	## Mode
4	cross-domain-inspiration
5	
6	## Slug
7	coe-stacks00sw
8	
9	## Iteration
10	201
11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	
14	Given a presentation `P : R → R[x₁,…,x_n] / (f₁,…,f_c) = S` whose Jacobian
15	matrix `(∂f_i/∂x_{σ(j)})_{i,j}` is invertible in `S` (i.e. a *submersive*
16	presentation), and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ S`, build a `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
17	witness for the relations `(f_j)` on the localisation
18	`A = (R[x_1,…,x_n])_{m'}` at the preimage maximal `m' = preimage(m)`.
19	
20	Abstractly: from a *non-degeneracy of the derivative* hypothesis at a point,
21	extract a *regular sequence* of the cutting-down relations in a local
22	neighbourhood of the point. The Mathlib-aligned target signature is
23	
24	```lean
25	lemma Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.relations_isRegular_in_localization
26	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] {ι σ : Type*}
27	    [Finite σ] (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
28	    (m : Ideal P.Ring) [m.IsMaximal]
29	    (mem : ∀ j, P.relation j ∈ m)
30	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra P.Ring A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
31	    RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A
32	      ((List.ofFn (fun (j : σ) => P.relation j)).map (algebraMap P.Ring A))
33	```
34	
35	The two natural choices for the witness's index list — `List.ofFn (P.relation ∘ e)`
36	for a chosen `e : Fin (Fintype.card σ) ≃ σ`, or `Finset.univ.toList.map P.relation`
37	— are interchangeable by `IsLocalRing.isRegular_of_perm`.
38

53	## Analogues found
54	
55	### Analogue A: `RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular.isRegular_of_isLocalizedModule_of_mem` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Flat:65`)
56	
57	- **Domain**: regular-sequence theory in commutative algebra (local + flat).
58	- **Same structural problem there**: given an `R`-module `M` with a *weakly*
59	  regular sequence `rs` on `M`, plus a localisation `M → N` at a prime `p`
60	  containing every element of `rs`, the image sequence is *honestly* regular
61	  on `N`. Exact statement:
62	
63	  ```lean
64	  theorem IsWeaklyRegular.isRegular_of_isLocalizedModule_of_mem
65	      [Nontrivial N] [Module.Finite S N] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule.AtPrime p f]
66	      {rs : List R} (reg : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) (mem : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ p) :
67	      IsRegular N (rs.map (algebraMap R S))
68	  ```
69	
70	- **Technique**: combines (i) flat base change for weakly-regular (`IsWeaklyRegular.of_flat_of_isBaseChange`)
71	  with (ii) the local-ring bridge `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`
72	  to upgrade weakly-regular to regular when the elements all lie in the maximal
73	  ideal of the localisation. The "nontriviality + Module.Finite" hypothesis
74	  is what makes the `top_ne_smul` half of `IsRegular` follow from being inside
75	  the maximal ideal.
76	- **Mapping to project**: this is *the* bridge once an `IsWeaklyRegular P.Ring (P.relations)`
77	  witness exists (at any prior level — `P.Ring`, or the localisation, or any
78	  intermediate quotient). The mapping:
79	  - `R` ← `P.Ring = MvPolynomial ι k`
80	  - `S` ← `A` (the localisation at `m`)
81	  - `M` ← `P.Ring` (used as a `P.Ring`-module)
82	  - `N` ← `A` (used as a `P.Ring`-module via `algebraMap`)
83	  - `p` ← `m` (the maximal ideal of `P.Ring`)
84	  - `rs` ← `(List.ofFn P.relation)`
85	  - `mem` ← the directive's `hmem` hypothesis.
86	
87	  After invoking this lemma, the IsRegular conclusion is exactly the target,
88	  modulo a `List.map (algebraMap …)` rewrite that the project already does
89	  in other places (`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard` transport).
90	- **Porting cost**: **none — direct invocation**. The hard work shifts to
91	  constructing the `IsWeaklyRegular P.Ring (P.relation)` premise, which is
92	  the next analogue.
93	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND. **This is the closing bridge.**
94	
95	### Analogue B: `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent` + `cotangentEquiv` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent:127,143`)
96	
97	- **Domain**: cotangent complex of a smooth presentation; commutative-algebra
98	  side of "smooth implies free conormal".
99	- **Same structural problem there**: encodes the *algebraic content* of
100	  Jacobian invertibility in the form Mathlib actually exposes. The exact
101	  statements:
102	
103	  ```lean
104	  noncomputable def cotangentEquiv : P.toExtension.Cotangent ≃ₗ[S] σ → S
105	  noncomputable def basisCotangent : Basis σ S P.toExtension.Cotangent
106	  lemma basisCotangent_apply (r : σ) :
107	      P.basisCotangent r = Extension.Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation r, P.relation_mem_ker r⟩
108	  ```
109	
110	  Reading this back: the images of `(P.relation r)_{r∈σ}` in
111	  `P.toExtension.Cotangent = I/I²` (where `I = ker(P.Ring →+* S)`) form an
112	  `S`-basis. This is the *cotangent-side* statement of Jacobian
113	  invertibility — equivalent in Mathlib to `jacobian_isUnit`.
114	- **Technique**: derived in `StandardSmoothCotangent.lean:116–144` from
115	  `linearIndependent_aeval_val_pderiv_relation` (relations' partial
116	  derivatives are linearly independent in `σ → S`) via the explicit
117	  isomorphism `P.toExtension.Cotangent ≃ₗ[S] σ → S` whose inverse is
118	  `(Cotangent.mk(relation r)) ↦ standardBasisVector r`. Crucially, this is
119	  the Mathlib-aligned encoding of "Jacobian invertible at every point of
120	  Spec S".
121	- **Mapping to project**: this is the substrate that *feeds* the
122	  `IsWeaklyRegular` construction. The route:
123	  1. From `basisCotangent`, the relations have linearly-independent images
124	     in `I/I²` over `S`.
125	  2. Localise: at the maximal `m` of `S`, the image is a basis of
126	     `I/I² ⊗_S S_m` over `S_m`, hence linearly independent in
127	     `(I·A)/(I·A)² ≅ I/I² ⊗_S Sₘ`.
128	  3. By `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace.span_image_eq_top_iff`, the images in
129	     the maximal ideal `m'·A` of `A` (where `A = P.Ring_{m'}`) generate
130	     `I·A` exactly. Combined with linear-independence: the relations form
131	     a *minimal* generating set of `I·A`.
132	  4. Apply the **classical Stacks 00NQ theorem** ("in a regular local ring,
133	     a sequence in `m` whose images in `m/m²` are linearly independent
134	     forms a regular sequence") to conclude `IsRegular A (relations)`.
135	- **Porting cost**: **medium**. Step 4 is the *one* Mathlib gap: the
136	  Matsumura "lin-indep cotangent ⇒ regular sequence" theorem is not in
137	  Mathlib (verified by `lean_leansearch` — only the conormal-free side
138	  via `basisCotangent` lives in Mathlib, the regular-sequence direction
139	  is a project-side build).
140	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND. **This is the technical bridge** for Step
141	  3 of the closure path; the porting target is the "lin-indep cotangent ⇒
142	  regular sequence in regular local ring" theorem.
143	
144	### Analogue C: `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence:510`)
145	
146	- **Domain**: local-ring + finite-module regular-sequence theory.
147	- **Same structural problem there**: in any local Noetherian ring with a
148	  finite nontrivial module, the *upgrade* from weakly-regular to regular
149	  is automatic for sequences in the maximal ideal. Exact statement:
150	
151	  ```lean
152	  lemma _root_.IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
153	      [IsLocalRing R] [Nontrivial M] [Module.Finite R M] {rs : List R}
154	      (h : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) :
155	      IsRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M rs
156	  ```
157	
158	- **Technique**: reduces `top_ne_smul` to `top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator`,
159	  using that `maximalIdeal ≤ jacobson` (which is *automatic* in a local
160	  ring). The bridge is purely local-ring algebra, no Noetherian
161	  hypothesis on `R` (only `Module.Finite R M`).
162	- **Mapping to project**: once `IsWeaklyRegular A (relations.map …)` is in
163	  hand on the localisation `A`, this bridge gives `IsRegular A (relations.map …)`

165	  Noetherian polynomial ring) and the relations lie in `m_A`. **However**,

169	  + upgrade in one call. Use this analogue only if a
170	  direct `IsWeaklyRegular A`-side construction (e.g. an inductive proof
171	  not factoring through the polynomial ring) is preferable.

174	  on whether the IsWeaklyRegular witness is built on `P.Ring` (use A) or

177	### Analogue D: `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule_of_isRegular` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`)
178	
179	- **Domain**: linear-algebra-side localisation invariance.
180	- **Same structural problem there**: linearly independent vectors over `R`
181	  map to linearly independent vectors over `R_S` when the localisation
182	  monoid `S` consists of regular elements. Exact statement:
183	
184	  ```lean
185	  theorem LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule_of_isRegular
186	      (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₛ) [IsLocalizedModule S f]
187	      {v : ι → M} (hli : LinearIndependent R v)
188	      (hreg : ∀ s ∈ S, IsRegular s) :
189	      LinearIndependent R (f ∘ v)
190	  ```
191	
192	- **Technique**: standard "tensor with `R_S` preserves linear
193	  independence" via flatness; uses that the localisation monoid acts by
194	  regular elements ⇒ the map `M → M_S` is injective on the span of `v`.
195	- **Mapping to project**: this is the *transport* step for the cotangent
196	  basis — it says that the basis property of `(P.relation r)_{r∈σ}` in
197	  `I/I²` survives localisation to `(I/I²)_{m}`. This is a substitute for
198	  step (2) in Analogue B's pipeline.

200	  `M = I/I²`, `Mₛ = (I/I²)_{m}`, `v = basisCotangent`. The "every element
201	  regular" hypothesis is automatic in a Noetherian local domain (the

206	### Cross-domain analogue E: `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.ImplicitFunction` (implicit function theorem)
207	
208	- **Domain**: differential geometry / functional analysis.
209	- **Same structural problem there**: given a `C^1` map `f : E → F` between
210	  Banach spaces with surjective derivative `df_a` at a point `a`, locally
211	  there is a *parametrisation* of `f⁻¹(f a)` by `ker(df_a)`, i.e. the
212	  zero locus is a regular submanifold cut out *transversally* by `f`'s
213	  components. Mathlib statement
214	  `HasStrictFDerivAt.implicitFunctionDataOfComplemented`:
215	
216	  ```lean
217	  -- (rough form)
218	  theorem implicit_function (h : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' a)
219	      (hsurj : LinearMap.range f' = ⊤) :
220	      ∃ φ : nbd a → nbd (f a), φ is a local C¹-section
221	  ```
222	
223	- **Technique**: contraction-mapping fixed point / Newton's method:
224	  iterate `x ↦ x − (df_a)⁻¹·f(x)` to construct the section. Linearisation
225	  + invertibility ⇒ local parametrisation.
226	- **Mapping to project**: structurally the *same* statement at the level
227	  of formal power series rings — Jacobian invertible at a point of `Spec S`
228	  ⇒ the relations cut out `Spec S` "linearly" in a formal neighbourhood,
229	  hence form a regular sequence. The contraction-mapping technique does
230	  *not* port directly (no contraction-mapping in the formal-power-series
231	  context inside Lean), but the *underlying conceptual core* is the same:
232	  "invertibility of the linearization is equivalent to regularity of the
233	  cut-out".
234	- **Porting cost**: **high** — the technique is not directly portable.
235	  Useful as a *sanity check* that the project's algebraic shape is the

238	  proof technique.

282	**Use the Analogue A + B + D composition.** Build the substantive Stacks
283	00NQ piece — "in a regular local ring of dim `n`, a sequence of `c`

43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
97	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
98	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
99	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
100	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
102	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
103	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
104	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
105	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
106	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
107	  % \varphi$."
108	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
109	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
110	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
111	  \[
112	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
113	  \]
114	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
115	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
116	  \[
117	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
118	  \]
119	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
120	
121	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
122	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
123	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
124	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
125	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
126	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
127	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
128	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
129	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
130	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{definition}
134	
135	\leanok
136	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
137	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
139	  
140	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
141	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
142	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
143	  % PDF page 22).
144	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
145	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
146	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
147	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
148	  conclusion).}
149	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
150	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
151	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
152	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
153	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
154	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
155	
156	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
157	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
158	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
159	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
160	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
161	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
162	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
163	  is empty) as a single line.
164	
165	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
166	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
167	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
168	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
169	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
170	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), $\eta \in
171	  \texttt{f.domain}\(. The encoding matches the standing \)\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
172	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
173	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
174	\end{definition}
175	
176	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
177	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
178	
179	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
180	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
181	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
182	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
183	
184	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
185	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
186	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
187	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
188	
189	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
190	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
191	
192	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
193	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
194	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
195	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
196	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
197	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
198	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
199	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
200	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
201	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
202	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
203	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
204	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
205	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
206	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
207	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
208	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
209	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
210	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
211	
212	\begin{lemma}
213	
214	\leanok
215	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
216	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
217	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
218	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
219	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
220	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
221	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
222	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
223	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
224	  \(S_M\)-module.
225	\end{lemma}
226	
227	\begin{proof}
228	  The canonical comparison map
229	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
230	  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
231	  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
232	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
233	  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
234	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
235	  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
236	  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
237	  the conclusion.
238	\end{proof}
239	
240	\begin{lemma}
241	
242	\leanok
243	  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
244	  the relative dimension]
245	  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
247	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
248	  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
249	  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
250	  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
251	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
252	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
253	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
254	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
255	\end{lemma}
256	
257	\begin{proof}
258	  Mathlib's
259	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
260	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
261	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
262	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
263	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
264	  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
265	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
266	  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
267	  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
268	  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
269	  ranks equal \(n\), so
270	  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
271	\end{proof}
272	
273	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
274	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
275	
276	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
277	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
278	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
279	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
280	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
281	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
282	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
283	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
284	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
285	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
286	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
287	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
288	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
289	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
290	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
291	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
292	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
293	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
294	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
295	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
296	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
297	named iter-199+ prover targets.
298	
299	\begin{lemma}
300	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
301	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
302	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
303	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
304	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
305	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
306	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
307	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
308	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
310	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
311	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
312	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
313	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
314	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
315	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
316	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
317	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
318	  \[
319	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
320	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
321	  \]
322	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
323	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
324	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
325	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
326	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
327	
328	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
329	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
330	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
331	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
332	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
333	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
334	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
335	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
336	\end{lemma}
337	
338	\begin{lemma}
339	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
340	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
342	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
343	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
344	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
345	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
346	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
347	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
348	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
349	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
350	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
351	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
352	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
353	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
354	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
355	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
356	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
357	  % $S'$-modules
358	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
359	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
360	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
361	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
362	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
363	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
364	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
365	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
366	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
367	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
369	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
370	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
371	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
372	  \[
373	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
374	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
375	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
376	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
377	  \]
378	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
379	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
380	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
381	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
382	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
383	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
384	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
385	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
386	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
387	  \[
388	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
389	  \]
390	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
391	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
392	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
393	\end{lemma}
394	
395	\begin{lemma}
396	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
397	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
398	  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
399	  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}
400	  % itself (see \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}); the iter-198
401	  % blueprint named a planned \texttt{...\_aux} extraction that has not
402	  % been created in the Lean file. Treat the lemma below as the in-body
403	  % closure pattern that the existing helper consumes once Stages 6.A and
404	  % 6.B land, rather than as a separately-pinned Lean declaration.
405	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
406	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
407	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
408	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
409	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
410	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
411	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
412	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
413	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
414	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
415	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
416	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
417	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
418	  dimension equals \(1\).
419	\end{lemma}
420	
421	\begin{proof}
422	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
423	  \begin{enumerate}
424	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
425	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
426	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
427	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
428	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
429	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
430	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
431	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
432	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
433	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
434	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
435	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
436	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
437	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
438	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
439	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
440	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
441	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
442	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
443	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
444	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
445	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
446	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
447	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
448	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
449	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
450	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
451	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
452	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
453	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
454	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
455	      the conclusion.
456	  \end{enumerate}
457	\end{proof}

571	\subsection{Stage 6.A iter-200 substrate: Stacks 00OE 3-step decomposition}
572	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
573	\textit{Source: \texttt{coe-stacks00oe} mathlib-analogist
574	cross-domain-inspiration recipe (iter-200, persistent
575	\texttt{analogies/coe-stacks00oe.md}); 7 axiom-clean
576	\texttt{private} substrate theorems landed in
577	\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L688--L800 under
578	\texttt{namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme}.}
579	
580	The iter-200 Lane COE prover phase decomposed Stage 6.A
581	(\ref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) into a
582	3-step Mathlib-chain. Steps 1 and 2 landed axiom-clean as
583	project-local \texttt{private} theorems; Step 3 landed in an
584	additive parameterised form awaiting the Jacobian-regular-sequence
585	witness for the relations of the smooth-algebra presentation.
586	
587	\paragraph{Step 1 --- Krull-dim \(\Rightarrow\) height (trivial,
588	\(\sim 1\) line).} Re-export of Mathlib's
589	\(\texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}\) under
590	the project-local name
591	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_eq\_height\_atPrime}\); reduces
592	the localised Krull-dim goal to a height-of-maximal-ideal goal.
593	
594	\paragraph{Step 2 --- Polynomial-ring maximal-ideal height
595	(\(\sim 60\) LOC).} For \(\texttt{MvPolynomial}\,(\texttt{Fin}\,n)\,k\)
596	with \(k\) a field, every maximal ideal has height \emph{exactly}
597	\(n\). Lower bound:
598	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_ge\_card\_of\_field}\),
599	induction on \texttt{Fin n} via \(\texttt{MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv}\)
600	+ Mathlib's \(\texttt{Polynomial.height\_eq\_height\_add\_one}\) plus
601	the Jacobson contraction
602	\(\texttt{Polynomial.isMaximal\_comap\_C\_of\_isJacobsonRing}\). Upper
603	bound:
604	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_le\_natCard\_of\_field}\)
605	via \(\texttt{Ideal.height\_le\_ringKrullDim\_of\_ne\_top}\) +
606	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim\_of\_isNoetherianRing}\) +
607	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_eq\_zero\_of\_field}\). Combined: the
608	\texttt{Fin n}-form
609	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_card}\) and the
610	general \texttt{[Finite ι]}-form
611	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_natCard}\) via
612	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.renameEquiv}\) +
613	\(\texttt{RingEquiv.height\_map}\). Capstone packaging the
614	Steps 1+2 composition for any localisation at a maximal ideal:
615	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_atMaximal\_MvPolynomial}\).
616	
617	\paragraph{Step 3 --- Drop by regular-sequence length (additive form
618	in hand, witness pending).} Step 3 generally:
619	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim}\,(R'_{m'} / (f_j)_{j \in \sigma}) +
620	 |\sigma| = \texttt{ringKrullDim}\,R'_{m'}\). The additive form is
621	landed as
622	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
623	via Mathlib's
624	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\),
625	parameterised over a hypothesis
626	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\,R'_{m'}\,\texttt{rs}\)
627	that is the iter-201+ residual substrate obligation. Note:
628	\(\texttt{WithBot}\,\mathbb N_\infty\) carries no \texttt{HSub}
629	instance, so the additive form is the natural API; a subtraction
630	form is not available.
631	
632	\paragraph{Iter-201+ Jacobian-regular-sequence witness recipe.} The
633	residual substrate of Stage 6.A is the Jacobian-criterion
634	regular-sequence witness for the relations of an
635	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\).
636	
637	\textbf{API choice} (per iter-201 \texttt{progress-critic route201}
638	flag): the project-local target predicate is Mathlib's
639	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\), \emph{not}
640	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\). Reason: the iter-200 substrate
641	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
642	(\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L807) consumes the strong predicate
643	\(\texttt{IsRegular}\) (via Mathlib's
644	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\));
645	the localised stalk \(R'_{m'}\) is local, so by Mathlib's
646	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\)
647	the two predicates are equivalent under the standing hypothesis that
648	the relations lie in the maximal ideal --- which holds because
649	the Jacobian-criterion conclusion is precisely that the relations
650	generate (a subset of) the maximal ideal at the closed point. The
651	recipe builds \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\) first (it is the
652	naturally-arising predicate from the Koszul-complex argument) and
653	then promotes to \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) via the bridge lemma; the
654	final consumer call uses the \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) form.
655	
656	The recipe (per the iter-200 prover handoff + the
657	\texttt{coe-stacks00oe} analogist + iter-201 \texttt{coe-stacks00sw}
658	analogist):
659	(i) extract the explicit
660	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\,\bar k\,
661	 \Gamma(X.\texttt{left}, V)\) from
662	\(\texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\) (Stage 6 sub-gap
663	(i) substrate iter-198); (ii) cite Stacks 00SW / 00OW + Matsumura
664	Thm 14.2: the relations \((f_j)_{j \in \sigma}\) of a submersive
665	presentation form a Koszul-regular sequence at the closed point
666	because the Jacobian matrix is invertible modulo the maximal ideal
667	\(\Leftrightarrow\) the images of \(f_j\) in \(\mathfrak m_A /
668	\mathfrak m_A^2\) are \(\kappa\)-linearly independent
669	\(\Rightarrow\) (Matsumura 14.2 / Stacks 00NQ on a regular local
670	ring) the \(f_j\) form a regular sequence in \(A\); (iii) bundle
671	as a 3-step chain:
672	\begin{itemize}
673	  \item \textbf{A1 — Matsumura helper} (\(\sim 30\)--\(50\) LOC,
674	    Mathlib upstream PR candidate): on a regular local Noetherian
675	    ring \((A, \mathfrak m)\) of Krull dim \(n\), a sequence
676	    \(f_1, \dots, f_c \in \mathfrak m\) whose images in
677	    \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) are \(\kappa\)-linearly
678	    independent forms an \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) sequence in \(A\).
679	    Proof: induction on \(c\), dim-drops-by-one via Mathlib's
680	    \(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_span\_singleton\_succ\_eq\_ringKrullDim}\)
681	    (in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/KrullDimension/Regular.lean}),
682	    \(\texttt{IsSMulRegular}\) on the non-zero-divisor \(f_1\) via
683	    \(\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} \Rightarrow \texttt{IsDomain}\) +
684	    \(f_1 \neq 0\) (since \(f_1 \notin \mathfrak m^2\)), then
685	    \(\texttt{isRegular\_cons\_iff}\) to descend.
686	  \item \textbf{A2 — Cotangent-localisation transport}
687	    (\(\sim 20\)--\(30\) LOC): transport
688	    \(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent}\)'s
689	    linear-independence statement from \(I/I^2\) over \(S\) to
690	    \(\mathfrak m_A / \mathfrak m_A^2\) over \(\kappa(\mathfrak m_A)\)
691	    on the localisation \(A = P.\texttt{Ring}_{m'}\). Uses
692	    \(\texttt{LinearIndependent.of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_isRegular}\)
693	    (in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/Module.lean}) +
694	    the conormal-localisation iso
695	    \((I/I^2) \otimes_S S_{\mathfrak m} \simeq
696	     (I \cdot A) / (I \cdot A)^2\).
697	  \item \textbf{A3 — Assembly} (\(\sim 10\)--\(15\) LOC): invoke
698	    A1 with the lin-indep premise from A2, then close via
699	    \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular.isRegular\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_mem}\)
700	    (in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/Regular/Flat.lean:65--73} ---
701	    DIRECT INVOCATION). Final declaration:
702	    \(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.relations\_isRegular\_in\_localization}\).
703	\end{itemize}
704	consuming Mathlib's existing
705	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.jacobian\_isUnit}\) +
706	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent}\) +
707	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.cotangentEquiv}\) +
708	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular.isRegular\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_mem}\) +
709	\(\texttt{LinearIndependent.of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_isRegular}\) +
710	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\);
711	(iv) plug into \texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}
712	+ \texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_atMaximal\_MvPolynomial} for
713	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim}\,S_{m} = n\) (with the standard-smooth
714	presentation's \texttt{relativeDimension} witness as \(n\)). Estimated
715	project-side cost: \(\sim 30-60\) LOC for (ii)+(iii); the full L1061
716	inline closure additionally requires scheme-to-algebra bridging
717	(extracting the presentation from
718	\(\Gamma(X.\texttt{left}, V)\) over \(\Gamma(\Spec, U) = \bar k\),
719	identifying the maximal ideal of \(\Gamma(X.\texttt{left}, V)\)
720	corresponding to \(z\)) which adds another \(\sim 30-50\) LOC. The
721	substrate landing trigger Lane T32 re-engagement (per
